docs.text:

    Installation

  • Interface Guide

    Interface

    Workflows

docs.title: Key Capabilities
docs.text:
Feature Description Takes Tree Unified hierarchy: Scene Groups → Scenes → View Layer Groups → View Layers → Versions. 6-Tier Cascade Property override resolution across six tiers — Global, Scene Group, Scene, View Layer Group, View Layer, View Layer Version. The most specific tier with a value wins. Tags & Rules Universal labelling system with tag-driven preset bundles applied through the cascade. Globals Panel Project-wide control centre — settings, presets overview, rules, tags, variants. Variant Switch Product variant system with per-scope material swapping, preset injection, and pool management. Rest State Auto-mirroring rest action system that preserves pristine default keyframes across animations. Batch Render Modal render engine (foreground or background) with a Smart Output token system for file naming. Render Presets 9 JSON-based preset categories (render, output, view layer, color, camera, world, material, file output, bookmarks) with 4-tier storage. View Layer Preview Live viewport thumbnails next to each View Layer in the tree. Process Monitor Runtime diagnostics — process tree, debug console, View Layer Switch profiler. Pie Menu Configurable navigation pie menu (Ctrl+Shift+C).

Blender Version

Takes for Blender requires Blender 5.0 or newer and builds on Blender's Slotted Action system. A few newer conveniences need 5.1+.

Need another language?

The wiki is published in English. Modern browsers can translate the whole page on the fly, free, with no extension to install:

  • Chrome / Edge / Brave — right-click anywhere on the page → Translate to…, or click the translate icon that appears in the address bar.
  • Firefox (118+) — right-click → Translate Page.
  • Safari (Big Sur / iOS 14+) — click the translate icon in the address bar.

Browser translation is private (processed locally where supported) and covers 100+ languages.

Created by Nikolaj Woroschilow

docs.title: Comparing Takes for Blender
docs.text:
If you've spent any time looking for ways to streamline a complex production pipeline in Blender, you've likely encountered several powerful Addons designed for scene management, lighting setups, or batch rendering.

Understanding the specific architectural intent of Takes for Blender compared to the rest of the ecosystem is crucial. Many users confuse Batch Rendering Queues with true Product Stage Management.

Here is an objective breakdown of where Takes fits into your pipeline compared to other popular industry tools.

docs.title: 1. The Context Managers
docs.text: These tools, like Takes, aim to save and toggle "states" of a scene (cameras, worlds, collections).

docs.title: Takes vs. Renderset (by polygoniq)
docs.text:
• Renderset's Approach: Renderset is a highly flexible "store anything" system. You manually right-click properties in Blender and save them to flat, independent "contexts."
  • Takes' Advantage (The Cascade Hierarchy): Instead of managing flat lists of contexts that must be updated individually when a client requests a global change, Takes uses a Tree Structure (The Cascade). Change the lighting environment at the root level, and all child takes inherit the change instantly. Takes also utilizes native Action Slots for true, non-destructive mesh/material animation swaps, eliminating the need to "hack" collections by duplicating objects.

docs.title: Takes vs. Polyviews
docs.text:
• Polyviews' Approach: A lightweight, straightforward tool for quickly rendering different camera angles with specific worlds and collection visibilities (popular in ArchViz).
  • Takes' Advantage: Built for high-end studio product visualization, Takes goes far beyond simple camera switching. It safely manages multi-material variants, per-tier action overrides, and is backed by a bulletproof safety system (the Watchlist) that prevents dangling data-loss during complex View Layer transitions.

docs.title: Takes vs. Photographer
docs.text:
• Photographer's Focus: A premium suite for physical camera properties (ISO, shutter speed, autofocus), light mixing, and optical lens effects. Photographer includes its own basic queue to batch render its cameras.
  • Takes' Advantage (They Complement Each Other): These tools are not competitors; they operate brilliantly together. Use Photographer to dial in your beautiful, photorealistic physical cameras and lights. Then, assign those perfect Photographer cameras to different nodes in your Takes Tree.

    Takes manages the Stage (materials, animations, cascading states). Photographer manages the Lens.

docs.title: Takes vs. Shot Manager
docs.text:
• Shot Manager's Focus: Designed for complex animation pipelines (e.g., short films, episodic rendering). It masters timeline frame-ranges, output node graphs, burn-ins, and NLA track management across sequential shots.
  • Takes' Advantage: Takes is built for product iteration and staging, not sequential timeline editing. If you need to edit a 50-shot animated short film sequence that plays back continuously, use Shot Manager. If you need to render out 5 different colorways of a shoe design, under 3 different lighting scenarios, acting out the exact same 5-second turntable spinning animation—Takes is vastly superior.

docs.title: Takes vs. Render+ (by Sinestesia)
docs.text:
• Render+'s Focus: The industry-standard tool strictly for queuing up batch render jobs, executing pre/post-render scripts, automatically shutting down PCs, and running headless renders.
  • Takes' Advantage: A batch renderer like Render+ only renders what you have manually built. It does not help you organize the scene states, manage material swaps, or build variants. Takes provides the UI and Architecture to visually orchestrate your scene states, and it includes a native, built-in Batch Renderer specifically tied to that tree structure.

docs.title: Summary: When to choose Takes
docs.text: Choose Takes for Blender when you need: 1. Non-destructive overrides: You don't want to duplicate massive geometry meshes into collection folders just to change a material or swap an animation. 2. The Cascade: You need high-level settings (like global lighting) to flow down to variations automatically. 3. Data Safety: You need a system that actively warns you if an animation data-block is on the verge of being silently lost during a transition. 4. All-in-One Interface: You want your scene manager, variant switcher, and batch render queue living inside a single, unified God-View panel.

docs.title: Takes for Blender — Corporate Identity (CI)
docs.text: This document establishes the foundational Brand Guidelines and Corporate Identity derived from the premium web UI mockup. It defines the rules for colors, typography, and UI language to ensure consistency across the addon, the documentation, and any marketing materials.

docs.title: 01. Brand Philosophy
docs.text: "Professional, Native, and Powerful." Takes for Blender is a robust stage-management pipeline. The visual identity must communicate stability, extreme technical competence, and a seamless native integration with Blender's own design language. It should feel like a premium, first-party engine upgrade.

docs.title: 02. Color Palette
docs.text: The brand strictly utilizes the iconic Blender colors, deeply saturated and contrasted against a modern dark-mode canvas.

docs.title: Core Brand Colors
docs.text: Color Hex Code Usage Blender Orange #e87d0d Primary Calls-to-Action, active states, highlights. Bright Orange #f5a623 Gradient peaks, glowing hover effects. Blender Blue #265787 Structural elements, secondary buttons, active tabs. Bright Blue #3a7bc8 Text gradients, link colors.

docs.title: Backgrounds & Surfaces
docs.text: Surface Hex Code Usage Deep Base #0a0a0c Absolute background of web pages (void). Surface Dark #121215 Default page background and panel bases. Surface Float #1e1e24 Elevated cards, dropdown menus, nested panels. Borders rgba(255,255,255, 0.05) Extremely subtle strokes on cards to define edges.

docs.title: 03. Typography
docs.text:
The identity relies on a high-contrast pairing of a geometric display font and a highly legible reading font.

  • Primary Headings (Display): Outfit
  • Use for massive hero sections, h1 - h3 tags, and logo text.
  • Weights: 600 (SemiBold), 800 (ExtraBold).
  • Why: The subtle geometry gives a modern, "3D software" vibe.
  • Body Text (Interface/Reading): Inter
  • Use for all paragraphs, navigation links, and UI labels.
  • Weights: 400 (Regular), 500 (Medium).
  • Why: Ultimate legibility at small UI scales.
  • Code & Technical: JetBrains Mono
  • Use for rendering code snippets, Python API docs, and command-line text.

docs.title: Glassmorphism (The Core Aesthetic)
docs.text: Headers, floating menus, and emphasized cards must utilize a dark, frosted glass effect. * Background: rgba(38, 87, 135, 0.2) (Tinted dark blue) * Blur: backdrop-filter: blur(16px) * Edge: 1px top outline rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08) to catch the "light".

docs.title: The Gradient Text Effect
docs.text: Massive headers (like "Takes for Blender") utilize a horizontal gradient block to draw the eye instantly. * linear-gradient(90deg, #3a7bc8 0%, #f5a623 100%)

docs.title: Shape Geometry & Borders
docs.text:
• Border Radiography:
  • 8px for large grid cards and containers.
  • 6px for primary buttons.
  • 4px for internal pills/tags.
  • Card Hover States:
  • Never stretch or warp. Instead, float upwards translateY(-4px).
  • Inject a soft, colored drop-shadow corresponding to the brand (e.g., 0 10px 25px rgba(232, 125, 13, 0.15)).

docs.title: 05. Imagery & Iconography
docs.text: The official "Takes for Blender" logo pairs the iconic 3D Clapperboard with strict original Blender coloring, emphasizing isometric structure over complex gradients.

docs.title: The Primary Logo (Flat Render)
docs.text: When dealing with non-SVG contexts or complex backgrounds, the solid-color flat vector rendering is used.

docs.title: The Structural Logo (SVG)
docs.text: The structural, core geometry of the logo uses pristine XML mathematical coordinates and pure Blender Hex hues.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
Is the wiki available in my language?

The wiki is in English. Modern browsers translate full web pages for free, with no extension required:

  • Chrome / Edge / Brave — right-click → Translate to…, or click the translate icon in the address bar.
  • Firefox (118+) — right-click → Translate Page.
  • Safari (Big Sur / iOS 14+) — click the translate icon in the address bar.

Browser translation is private (processed locally where supported) and covers 100+ languages.

What Blender version do I need?

Takes for Blender requires Blender 5.0 or newer — it relies on Blender's Slotted Action system. A few newer conveniences need 5.1+.

Does it work with EEVEE and Cycles?

Yes. The cascade and batch render systems are engine-agnostic. Render presets capture engine-specific settings automatically.

Can I use it with other addons?

Generally yes. Takes for Blender uses standard Blender API and does not monkey-patch core functionality. If you encounter conflicts, please report them.

docs.title: Takes & Cascade
docs.text:
What happens to my animation when I switch View Layers?

Objects snap to their Rest State (neutral pose) if they don't have animation on the target View Layer. If they do, the cascade action is applied automatically.

Why is my cascade icon dimmed?

A dimmed icon means the value is inherited from a parent tier. A bright icon means it's explicitly set at that tier. Alt+Click to clear an override.

Can I have different frame ranges per View Layer?

Frame ranges are a Scene-level property in Blender. Use different Scenes if you need different frame ranges.

docs.title: Inspector
docs.text:
Why is my list red?

A red highlight in the watchlist indicates a dangling action warning — an object has animation data that isn't managed by the cascade. Pin the object or enable Auto-Assign to resolve it.

What's the difference between Managed and Pinned?

Managed objects follow the cascade — their action is assigned automatically. Pinned objects keep their own action regardless of View Layer switches.

docs.title: Batch Render
docs.text:
Why didn't my render start?

Check that:

  1. The file is saved (required for background mode).
  2. A camera is assigned in the cascade.
  3. The batch system isn't stuck (Alt+Click the Render button to reset).
Can I render only specific View Layers?

Yes. The batch queue is built from each View Layer's render-toggle icon (the camera icon at the right of its tree row) — enable it on the takes you want, then pick a Selected Takes scope in the render menu. Tree multi-select drives delete / group / preview operations and has no effect on the render queue.

Where are my renders saved?

Output paths are determined by the Smart Output pattern. Check Properties > Output to see the current pattern. Token resolution happens at render time.

docs.title: Variant Switch
docs.text:
Can I mix Swap and Pool modes?

Each Part uses one mode at a time. Different Parts within the same Product can use different modes. See Variant Switch for what the two modes do.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
The addon isn't showing up after installation.

Make sure you installed from the .zip without extracting. Check Blender's console (Window > Toggle System Console) for errors.

I'm getting crashes during View Layer switches.

This usually indicates a conflict with another addon's depsgraph handler. Try disabling other addons to isolate the issue. Enable debug logging and share the log file when reporting.

My custom keyboard shortcuts disappear after enabling the addon and restarting Blender.

Fixed in the current build. If you're on an older version, update to the latest. The cause was a registration pattern that polluted Blender's user keyconfig with entries flagged is_user_defined=True. On the next Blender restart, Blender's "rebuild user keyconfig from addon keyconfig" pass — which fires for any keymap the addon registers into (Window, 3D View) — would drop every is_user_defined=True entry in those keymaps, regardless of which operator it referenced. Custom shortcuts you'd added by hand to those keymaps got wiped along with the addon's own internal entries.

The current build registers shortcuts only in the addon keyconfig and lets Blender auto-mirror into the user keyconfig — the mirrored entries aren't user-defined, so the rebuild-and-wipe doesn't trigger.

If your customs were already wiped by a previous version, you'll need to set them again; Blender doesn't keep a recovery copy of userpref.blend.

� �

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

Legend: 🟢 confirmed working in-app  ·  🔴 added but not yet verified in-app

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. 🟢 The add-on's labels and tooltips can follow Blender's language setting — German is the first pilot language, starting with a handful of labels while the full translation is filled in.
  2. 🟢 Internal: a developer tool gathers every piece of add-on text for translation — no behavior change.
  3. 🟢 The add-on's side panel now shows up in German — panel titles, tab and section headers, list menus and keyframe pies.
  4. 🟢 Internal: an automated check catches wording changes that leave a translation behind, and flags new text that has no translation decision yet — no behavior change.
  5. 🟢 Nearly the whole interface now speaks German — preferences, buttons, menus, warnings and short tooltips; only the longer explanation texts are still English.
  6. 🟢 The Autokey button now handles Blender 5.2's "Only Insert Available" setting for you — it asks once for permission, turns the setting off while Autokey is on so fresh takes key immediately, restores it after, and backs off with a warning instead if you manage the setting yourself.
  7. 🟢 The German translation is complete — every label, tooltip and message the add-on shows now has German wording, so switching Blender to Deutsch no longer drops you back into English mid-panel.
  8. 🟢 Messages the add-on builds on the fly now translate too — every dialog heading, warning header, confirmation and status line composes its German around the inserted name or count, including correct singular and plural wording ("1 Kaskaden-Abweichung" / "3 Kaskaden-Abweichungen").

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Internal: removed a leftover unused helper from the Rest State cache — no behavior change.
  2. 🟢 Internal: a test that assumed English datablock names now passes on non-English Blender installations — no behavior change.
  3. 🟢 Internal: the add-on's info-box texts can now carry manual line breaks — no visible change until a text uses one.
  4. 🟢 Internal: 18 tooltip and info-box texts moved into the central texts folder, so their wording lives in one editable place — no visible change.
  5. 🟢 Internal: a further 143 property tooltips moved into the central texts folder — no visible change.
  6. 🟢 Internal: text that lives in the central texts folder is now picked up for translation; roughly 500 already-centralized strings had been invisible to the translation collector.
  7. 🟢 Internal: every operator tooltip now lives in the central texts folder instead of being taken from a developer comment in the code — same wording on screen, but the text is editable in one place and translatable for the first time.
  8. 🟢 Menu tooltips (the dropdowns' hover text) are now centralized and translated too — they previously showed only in English because they lived in the code rather than the texts folder.
  9. 🟢 The last multi-line property tooltips are now centralized in the texts folder too — no visible change (they were already translated), just editable in one place. Only a handful of tooltips built at runtime remain in code.
  10. 🟢 The very last tooltips built at runtime are now centralized too, so every piece of UI text lives in the texts folder — and the two storage-warning boxes translate as well.
  11. 🟢 Status and error messages the add-on shows after an action can now be translated too (Blender never translates those automatically) — all 651 are routed through the translation system; the German wording is filled in next.
  12. 🟢 Status and error messages now appear in German too — all 525 are translated, so a German user sees German feedback after every action, not just German buttons and tooltips.
  13. 🟢 Tooltips, setting descriptions and info boxes read cleaner across the whole add-on — stray line breaks and double spaces are gone, punctuation and terminology are consistent (View Layer, Alt+Click), developer notes no longer leak into hover text, and the German translations follow the new wording.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Clearing an object's keyframes no longer traps it: moving it afterwards stays put instead of snapping back to rest after every move, objects sharing the same take are left alone, and the snap that fires on a genuine keyframe deletion lands on the actual rest pose instead of a stale spot.
  2. 🟢 The "Auto-snap on Keyframe Clear" preference now actually turns the automatic snap off — it previously did nothing.
  3. 🟢 The New Slot button in a datablock's action popover creates the slot — it previously always failed with a Python error.
  4. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual on the two autokey-setup buttons now opens the section that documents them, instead of dropping you on the wiki home page.
  5. 🟢 The two autokey-setup buttons grey out when the add-on's preferences can't be read, instead of staying clickable and quietly doing nothing.
  6. 🟢 Buttons in the add-on now translate too, not just panel headings — the Context and Inspector tabs read Kontext and Inspektor in German.
  7. 🟢 The custom-token preset picker reads like the other preset rows — a searchable dropdown, plus create, rename and delete buttons once a preset is chosen.
  8. 🟢 Info boxes now show in your language — the explanatory text inside tip and warning boxes was staying English in a translated interface because it was word-wrapped before translation; it's now translated first.
  9. 🟢 Starting a batch render on Blender 5.2 no longer crashes Blender instantly — a Blender 5.2 bug hands add-ons an empty render-statistics message the moment a render starts, taking the whole application down before the add-on can react; the add-on now stays off that channel on 5.2 and the progress bars use the time-based estimate instead.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Automatic View-Layer Preload can open the progress panel for you.
  2. 🟢 Read the changelog on the public wiki, under a new What's New section alongside the roadmap.
  3. 🟢 A token/value toggle in the Build Syntax popover switches the grid between token names and their live values.
  4. 🟢 A naming or output field's dropdown menu now lists its most-used tokens to insert with one click, plus Clear Last Token and Clear All.
  5. 🟢 A search bar above the Preferences tabs filters the naming settings — non-matching options dim while matches stay highlighted.
  6. 🟢 A "Show Tip Boxes" switch (Preferences → Interface → Confirmations → Info Boxes) hides the add-on's explanatory tip boxes.
  7. 🟢 A "Show Warning Details" switch hides the explanatory text inside warning panels — the warning and its fix buttons stay.
  8. 🟢 An ✕ on a tip or warning box hides that whole category, after a confirmation.
  9. 🟢 The Build Syntax sheet offers Blender's own filepath tokens — scene, camera, resolution, blend-file name, frame number and more — on render-path fields, alongside the add-on's tokens.
  10. 🟢 Every naming and output field that accepts tokens now has its own Build Syntax button, seeded from that field.
  11. 🟢 File Output node Directory and File Name fields now carry the same dropdown menu as the Properties Output rows — Build Syntax, Make Absolute/Relative, insert a token, and Clear Last / Clear All.
  12. 🟢 The Build Syntax popover flags a render directory that isn't a writable root — it must start with // or an absolute path — with an absolute/relative badge and Make Absolute / Make Relative.
  13. 🟢 The Properties Output and File Output node directory fields turn red with the reason when the path isn't a writable root.
  14. 🟢 The Build Syntax popover edits a private draft — a red Apply flag appears beside the pattern field as soon as it differs from the field's live value, and only clicking that flag writes the pattern back.
  15. 🟢 The Build Syntax popover flags a pattern that leads, trails, or doubles a separator as invalid syntax.
  16. 🟢 The Build Syntax popover's dropdown menu offers Copy, Make Absolute/Relative, Reset to Default, Clear Last Token and Clear All.
  17. 🟢 A chip-visibility toggle in the Build Syntax header hides the movable chips for a compact, field-only layout.
  18. 🟢 The Context panel warns when a naming template has a wrong-format or invalid token, with one-click fixes; a broken template falls back to its default so names keep generating.
  19. 🟢 The render queue remembers each take's recent render times together with the settings they were measured at, and rescales its estimates when resolution or samples change.
  20. 🟢 A Value Lock button in the navigation header protects the scene's unkeyed values — objects, materials, lights, the world, and everything else that can hold a static value. While it's on, nudging an un-animated value on your selected objects snaps it back to what it was when you locked — guarding against accidental global changes that would apply at every frame. Keyframed (animated) values stay fully unlocked, and deliberately keying a value takes it out of the lock. Autokey pauses while the lock is on (its button dims) and returns on unlock. Take switching keeps working while locked, on any view layer, with or without an action.
  21. 🟢 A Rest State Mode button in the navigation header switches every view layer to show the rest baseline — compare it against your work or adjust it in place, then click again to return; tree assignments and renders always keep the real actions, and autokey pauses while it's on.
  22. 🟢 A status banner near the top of the 3D viewport shows a colored pill for each active mode you choose to surface — Autokey, Value Lock, Rest Mode, Still Mode, Timeline Sync — plus a progress bar for each running background task (view-layer preload, batch render), so a mode or a busy pause is visible at a glance when the Takes panel is closed; pick which mode pills appear, place the banner at any corner or edge with an X/Y pixel nudge and an opacity slider to fine-tune, hide the modes or the progress rows independently, or turn it off under Preferences → Interface → Overlay.
  23. 🟢 A Still & Animation mode, set globally from the navigation header or per take. Still pins a take's timeline to a single frame — start and end both at 0 — so you can build and light a still without scrubbing off or dropping a keyframe on the wrong frame; Animate gives a take its own start/end range; Default inherits from the level above. Click a take's mode icon for a quick Still / Animate / Clear dropdown, or Shift-click for a Timeline Options popover that sets the range and a pinnable default frame the playhead jumps to when you switch to that take — a viewport convenience that never changes what renders and is overridden while Timeline Sync is on. Renders of a Still take render frame 0, autokey stays on since any key just lands on frame 0, changing one take's mode never moves another take's timeline, and it works alongside Value Lock and Rest State Mode.
  24. 🟢 A Pacing setting (Gentle / Balanced / Fast) chooses how hard the view-layer preloader rests between layer builds.
  25. 🟢 A new Icon Sheet browses every Blender icon by category or search, copies an icon's name to the clipboard when you click it, and keeps a Recent tab of the icons you've used most recently; open it from a hotkey you assign — or a button — under Preferences → Developer → Utilities.
  26. 🟢 The switch profiler shows a Camera Visibility row — whether the camera automation fired on that switch, and how long it took.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 The View-Layer Preload panel now behaves like the other navigation warning panels.
  2. 🟢 "Snap Active to Rest" is grayed out in the keyframe pie when the active object has nothing to snap back to.
  3. 🟢 Internal: annotated Takes code with auditor-awareness markers for intentional whole-file data scans and display-only icon maps, so the auditor recognizes them as by-design patterns, not bugs.
  4. 🟢 Hovering a token in the Build Syntax popover shows what it is and what it resolves to right now.
  5. 🟢 Internal: the Build Pattern popover and the node-editor token panel now share one token grid — no behavior change.
  6. 🟢 The naming separator character now lives under Preferences → Pie & Misc → Tokens.
  7. 🟢 Internal: the Smart Output token engine moved into its own Smart Syntax feature, separate from Batch Render — no behavior change.
  8. 🟢 The Preferences naming tab is now titled "Syntax".
  9. 🟢 The add-on's explanatory boxes wrap long text to fit the panel instead of cutting it off, and carry a header.
  10. 🟢 Internal: the Syntax rename now extends to the code identifiers — no behavior change.
  11. 🟢 Tokens that duplicate a Blender filepath token now use Blender's name (e.g. resolution_x, blend_name), and the add-on's own custom tokens are spelled out in full (viewlayer_group, resolution_percentage); patterns using the old names still work.
  12. 🟢 The Output labels read "Directory" and "File Name" instead of "Directory Pattern" / "File Name Pattern".
  13. 🟢 The Build Syntax popover shows the editable field on top with the movable chips below at all times, instead of switching between a field view and a chip view.
  14. 🟢 Separator rows in the token grid show both the character and its name, and searching the grid dims non-matching tokens in place instead of reflowing it.
  15. 🟢 Internal: cleared the remaining add-on code-quality audit findings — operator readiness guards, annotated never-fail error handlers, and a dead helper removed — no behavior change.
  16. 🟢 The batch render ETA now corrects itself while a batch runs — once the first take finishes, the remaining time reflects how this run is actually performing.
  17. 🟢 Calibrate Render Times probes each take at a reduced resolution with the real sampling and denoising settings, so estimates respect adaptive sampling.
  18. 🟢 Background batch renders show the render engine's own remaining-time prediction for the take currently rendering.
  19. 🟢 Switching takes in large scenes now completes in about half the time.
  20. 🟢 Switching takes right after an undo or redo is now just as fast as any other switch.
  21. 🟢 A background preload now pauses while you render, play animation, or navigate, and paces itself to keep the viewport smooth.
  22. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual now opens the exact wiki section for a control — slot management, keyframe pies, drift panels, preset lifecycle, tree editing and more — instead of the top of its feature page.
  23. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual on any add-on setting in Preferences now opens that setting's own manual page — Interface, Debug, Syntax, Automations, Pie & Misc, or Viewport Sync — instead of the Preferences overview.
  24. 🟢 Custom-token preset, category and token buttons, the Value Lock button and the Rest State button grey out when there is nothing for them to act on.
  25. 🟢 Internal: removed leftover unused code from the take-switching core — no behavior change.
  26. 🟢 Internal: code identifiers and the naming standard aligned with the naming-conformance audit — no behavior change.
  27. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual now jumps to the exact wiki section for around 670 more controls — including every Preferences setting, tree and group editing, presets, tokens and the panel toggles — instead of the top of a page.
  28. 🟢 The rest-drift indicator also flags keyframed controls nudged off their pose (not just unkeyed strays) and shows in Rest State Mode; Snap sends each drifted control back to what the current mode expects — its own keyframe in a normal take, the rest pose in Rest State Mode — and driver-driven channels are left out so they don't read as phantom drift.
  29. 🟢 The Rest State Mode button is grayed out until a Rest State action exists, and hovering it explains how to create one — so you can't switch into a mode that would show nothing.
  30. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual on the Still Mode buttons opens the new Still Mode manual page at the matching section.
  31. 🟢 The navigation header's mode buttons are ordered Rest, Still, Value Lock, Autokey, Timeline Sync from left to right.
  32. 🟢 Timeline Sync switches off and greys out whenever the active take is a Still — set from the global toggle, per take, or by switching onto a Still take — and returns to its previous state when the take goes back to animating.
  33. 🟢 While Rest State Mode is on, the action icons in the Takes tree are grayed out — clicking, Shift-clicking or Alt-clicking them no longer opens the action dropdown or changes an assignment, since the mode is showing the rest baseline; the rest action's own ghost picker stays available.
  34. 🟢 A preload build now waits until the viewport is completely still, so it no longer grabs Blender the moment you stop navigating.
  35. 🟢 The preload panel shows a total progress bar, which layer is building right now, and each finished layer's build time.
  36. 🟢 The preload settings in Preferences line up as label-and-field rows, matching the naming-template style.
  37. 🟢 The layer being preloaded shows a filling progress bar on its own row in the preload panel, the ETA sits in the panel's title, and the viewport overlay carries the same current-layer bar under its preload row.
  38. 🟢 Preloading no longer stutters the viewport between layer builds, and a build waits for about a second of complete stillness instead of firing the moment you pause.
  39. 🟢 The percentages in the viewport overlay line up in a right-aligned column.
  40. 🟢 The brief pause right after a layer finishes preloading no longer lands on your first movement — it is absorbed while you are still.
  41. 🟢 Value Lock switches itself on while view layers preload — protecting your own accidental nudges during the run — and can't be switched off until the run finishes or you cancel it.
  42. 🟢 The view-layer Preload settings now sit in their own Preferences section.
  43. 🟢 More Preferences dropdowns — duplicate handling, syntax brackets, render version scope, preset changes, the viewport anchor and the Ctrl+N add defaults — use the same right-aligned label and clean dropdown as the naming rows.
  44. 🟢 Any Preferences section folds its whole group open or closed with Shift-click, with a solo mode that keeps just one open — now across the Token tab, the Configuration Inspector and the keyframe-pie sub-menus.
  45. 🟢 Preferences notices about missing storage paths, project-preferences state and the editor-scope hint use the add-on's word-wrapped info box, and the Configuration Inspector's Legend is a dismissable info box you can hide.
  46. 🟢 Internal: Preferences row spacing unified on shared layout constants — no behavior change.
  47. 🟢 The Preferences "Debug" tab is now called "Developer", with a new Utilities sub-tab.
  48. 🟢 While a take is Still, the timeline scrub bar can't move the playhead off the still frame, and your own playhead-limit setting returns when the take animates again.
  49. 🟢 The category icon picker opens on the tab of the icon the category currently uses, and a copy button beside the current icon's name puts that name on the clipboard.
  50. 🟢 Internal: the test suite now blocks any new architecture debt automatically — no behavior change.
  51. 🟢 Internal: the take-switching core talks to the interface through announcements instead of direct wiring — no behavior change, switching stays just as fast.
  52. 🟢 Internal: panel refresh signals now flow through one shared registry instead of direct wiring — no behavior change.
  53. 🟢 Internal: three large code files reorganized into smaller focused modules — no behavior change.
  54. 🟢 Switching takes no longer slows down as a file accumulates cameras while Camera Visibility Automation is on.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Clearing all keyframes at the current frame now affects only the scene you're working in.
  2. 🟢 The View-Layer Preload panel no longer gets stuck open after a preload finishes.
  3. 🟢 A view layer you've switched to no longer shows as "Not Ready" in the preload panel after you move on.
  4. 🟢 The public roadmap is now a live board that keeps itself up to date, with a clearer layout.
  5. 🟢 Switching view layers now snaps every object back to its Rest State, not only the ones you had recently selected.
  6. 🟢 Switching between two view layers that share the same action no longer resets animated objects to their Rest values.
  7. 🟢 The naming-template rows in Preferences show a single clean dropdown arrow instead of a doubled one.
  8. 🟢 The File Name preview resolves correctly even when the Directory is invalid, and an invalid directory no longer hides the rest of the Output panel.
  9. 🟢 Reset and Clear actions in the Build Syntax popover update the field, chips and preview immediately instead of after a mouse move.
  10. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual opens the matching feature page for around a hundred more controls, instead of the wiki home.
  11. 🟢 The Take Name naming template kept reverting to an invalid value on fresh starts on developer machines where the test suite had run.
  12. 🟢 Calibrating render times no longer produces multi-minute estimates for takes that actually render in seconds.
  13. 🟢 A background preload no longer overwrites camera, world, or action choices you made by hand.
  14. 🟢 Scenes shown in a second window are no longer switched around by a background preload.
  15. 🟢 The View-Layer switch profiler refreshes after an undo or redo, and phases an undo doesn't re-run read "skipped" instead of showing blank.
  16. 🟢 The public wiki publishes again on every push — its build had been rejecting a documented render operator and the roadmap board's links to internal planning notes.
  17. 🟢 Moving an animated object and then switching between view layers that share the same action now snaps it back to its animated pose — previously the accidental move stuck, because only an action change triggered the restore.
  18. 🟢 Internal: the automated test suite no longer reads the developer's personal settings mid-run — 19 long-standing intermittent test failures are gone.
  19. 🟢 Deleting or unlinking the Rest State action while Rest State Mode is on now switches the mode off by itself — autokey and the Value Lock come back, the view layers return to their real actions, and the button grays out until you assign a rest action again. Exiting the mode normally still restores both autokey and the lock, just as it pauses them on the way in.
  20. 🟢 A background preload no longer leaks another take's action onto your current take when you click an object afterwards.
  21. 🟢 While a preload run is live, working in the viewport no longer stutters between layer builds, and a stray action can no longer land on your current take mid-run.
  22. 🟢 The "Pacing" preload option is now found by the Preferences search.
  23. 🟢 The viewport overlay mode icons dim while searching Preferences.
  24. 🟢 Loading saved settings no longer empties pie-menu slots when a saved assignment collides with a newly shipped slot default.
  25. 🟢 Shared or Local settings storage with no folder chosen now shows a warning that the add-on default file is in use, instead of falling back silently.
  26. 🟢 Dragging the timeline playhead while a Still take was active could crash Blender.
  27. 🟢 Searching the Token Builder grid now lights up every matching token — tokens with nothing to show right now (an empty stamp note, camera tokens with no camera) previously stayed dim even when they matched.
  28. 🟢 The Preferences Data tab opens without crashing.
  29. 🟢 The overflow indicator on a cascade column that's showing variants no longer errors when clicked.
  30. 🟢 The Select button beside a camera field selects the camera even when it comes from a view-layer, group, or global override — not only the scene's own camera.

docs.title: Removed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 The token reference browser under Preferences → Naming — its tokens are now in the Build Syntax popover.
  2. 🟢 The unlock step for naming templates — every naming template in Preferences is editable directly.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. 🟢 The Smart Output directory shows whether its path is absolute or relative, and lets you switch between them.
  2. 🟢 Build Smart Output naming patterns by clicking tokens instead of copying them one at a time.
  3. 🟢 Two path tokens — // (relative root) and ../ (up one folder) — for building directory patterns.
  4. 🟢 View-Layer Preload — the first switch to each take no longer stalls.
  5. 🟢 See how long a view-layer switch really takes, not just the add-on's share.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Internal: operators, menus, and panels that shared a file are now separated so each file holds a single kind — no behavior change.
  2. 🟢 Internal: operator code modules renamed for naming consistency — no behavior change.
  3. 🟢 Internal: preference setting-group classes renamed for naming consistency — no behavior change.
  4. 🟢 Internal: three further files that mixed operators with menus or lists were separated — no behavior change.
  5. 🟢 Internal: three more files that mixed a data list with its on-screen list were separated — no behavior change.
  6. 🟢 Internal: four more files that mixed operators/menus/lists (or a data group with its list) were separated — no behavior change.
  7. 🟢 Internal: the two cascade picker dropdown menus were separated from their operators — no behavior change.
  8. 🟢 The "Reference State" feature is now called "Rest State" throughout the add-on, docs, and wiki — same behaviour, clearer name. All operator IDs and properties were renamed accordingly.
  9. 🟢 Each Smart Output pattern row keeps its actions in one ▾ drop-down beside the field.
  10. 🟢 The Smart Output bracket style moved into Preferences.
  11. 🟢 The Standard / Link / Compositor output-mode buttons now sit beside the Smart Output toggle and gray out when Smart Output is off, instead of disappearing.
  12. 🟢 Separator tokens share one definition and show a clear glyph.
  13. 🟢 The Build Pattern popover previews each token's value and dims the ones that don't apply here.
  14. 🟢 With Smart Output on, the Output panel no longer shows the redundant "synced from above" banner or a grayed-out copy of Blender's filepath field.
  15. 🟢 Shift+Click a group chevron to expand or collapse a whole list at once.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Switching between view-layer versions no longer crashes Blender during heavy property editing.
  2. 🟢 The navigation and F12 pie-menu enable toggles no longer raise an error during add-on registration.
  3. 🟢 The autokey-mirror handler is now properly suppressed during view-layer switches.
  4. 🟢 Sync Brackets also repairs mismatched bracket pairs.
  5. 🟢 Make Absolute resolves ../ folder-up steps instead of leaving them in the path.
  6. 🟢 A Rest Action whose action was deleted now loads showing the name in red instead of going blank.
  7. 🟢 The Context tree view now repopulates every time you open a file, not just the first time in a session.
  8. 🟢 The VL Switch Profiler's categories collapse, and stay collapsed.
  9. 🟢 Editing a view layer that uses the Rest Action no longer snaps your changes back.
  10. 🟢 Buttons that can't do anything now grey out instead of staying clickable.
  11. 🟢 The add-on's license is declared in the format the Blender Extensions platform requires.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. 🔴 Outdated or incompatible preset files are detected instead of loading silently.
  2. 🔴 A "Check Coverage" report lists preset values that can't be stored.
  3. 🔴 Driven and NLA-only values now show up in the Channels panel.
  4. 🟢 Choose what happens to a duplicated object's animation.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Internal: four oversized modules split into focused submodules — no behavior change.
  2. 🟢 The Debug Monitor opens large log files faster.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Pie "Reset to default" buttons now work.
  2. 🟢 Switching between view layers with no action reliably snaps objects back to the rest pose — including right after you edit the reference.
  3. 🟢 The watchlist's View-Layer-switch progress icons reflect what actually ran.
  4. 🟢 Bookmark presets now show a change-warning and Save/Revert.
  5. 🟢 Bookmarked values stay visible in the Channels panel even without keyframes.
  6. 🔴 Material, World and File-Output presets are no longer silently skipped by preset operations.
  7. 🟢 The Inspector channel search filters live as you type.
  8. 🔴 Duplicating an action no longer leaves stray rest-pose slots behind.
  9. 🔴 Project presets resolve to the same folder at startup and after a reload.

docs.title: Tooling
docs.text:
Maintainer-only — the /stats code-quality dashboard, not part of the shipped add-on.

  1. Two new audits: CORR-08 Preset Dispatch Exhaustiveness and CORR-09 Channel Coverage.
  2. INFO-05 Naming-Convention enforcement.
  3. Dashboard layout controls.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Keyframe pies gain Still and Animation sub-modes with scope.
  2. 🟢 Ctrl+N can create a tree item directly instead of opening the add menu.
  3. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual on any Takes control opens the user guide.
  4. 🟢 Solo mode for the preferences accordions.
  5. 🟢 Start a batch render straight from the Takes tree.
  6. 🟢 The Takes-tree stats bar shows how many view layers will render, and updates live during a render.
  7. 🟢 Filter the Takes tree by render icon.
  8. 🟢 Pick a specific scene to render from the render menu.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Every Preferences panel now uses the same collapsible accordion layout.
  2. 🟢 Shift+Click any section chevron to expand or collapse the whole menu at once.
  3. 🟢 One render button with a Foreground/Background toggle replaces the two separate render buttons.
  4. 🟢 The F12 Render Pie slot dropdown is grouped and easier to read.
  5. 🟢 The Retarget control moved into the tree settings menu.
  6. 🟢 The preview-thumbnail options in the tree settings menu are now collapsible once previews are enabled.
  7. 🟢 Pie-configurator reset buttons now read "Reset to default."

docs.title: Removed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 The "Reset Camera Visibility" button is gone from the tree settings menu.
  2. 🟢 Three redundant keyframe-pie actions are gone.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Undo/redo re-applies the right action when you switch View Layer between scenes.
  2. 🟢 Undo no longer snaps objects to the rest pose.
  3. 🟢 Adding View Layers and copying scenes no longer crashes Blender.
  4. 🟢 Cross-scene undo reliably keeps each View Layer's take assignment.
  5. 🟢 "Active Layer Only" no longer throws on an unsaved file.
  6. 🟢 Clear Pycache and Reload Addon now work on an unsaved file.

docs.title: Tooling
docs.text:
Maintainer-only — the /stats code-quality dashboard, not part of the shipped add-on.

  1. Manual-Map Coverage audit.
  2. Paste a copied Blender path into the audit search to jump to an element.
  3. The dashboard Refresh button works again and moved into the Settings menu.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. Delete a datablock outright from any cascade icon with Ctrl+Shift+Alt+Click. The chord opens a confirmation dialog naming the datablock, listing every other take / View Layer / scene it's still assigned to, its total file user count, and a fake-user warning — then unassigns it everywhere and deletes it. Works for Actions, Worlds, Compositors, and Cameras at every tier (a Camera deletes the object but keeps its camera data). Plain / Shift / Alt clicks are unchanged; the delete is undoable.

  2. Link-and-Assign confirmation when picking a camera that's missing from some scenes. Picking a camera at the Global or Scene Group tier where the camera isn't linked into every covered scene now opens a small dialog listing the missing scenes and asking what to do: link the camera into those scenes (preserving its existing collection placement) and assign, just assign and let the cascade silently skip the unresolvable scenes, or cancel. Removes the previous trap where a cross-scene camera pick would either silently fail on some scenes or — worse — get wiped at the source the moment the active scene couldn't resolve it.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. Camera picker dropdowns now match each tier's reach. The Scene, View Layer, VL Group, and VL Version pickers list only the cameras linked into that target scene — picks from outside got silently auto-cleared by the cascade anyway. Global and Scene Group pickers still list every camera in the file, but cameras not linked into all covered scenes get a red icon and a (missing in N scene(s)) suffix; a warning row below the dropdown names exactly which scenes the chosen camera won't reach.

  2. Global and Scene Group camera assignments survive scene switches. Previously, switching to a scene where the chosen camera wasn't linked silently wiped the assignment for every scene the tier covers — destroying the deliberate cross-scene default the user had set up. The cascade now keeps cross-scene assignments intact and just skips applying them for the unreachable scenes; only the scene-bound tiers (Scene / VL / VLG / VLV) still self-heal when a pick goes stale.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. Output, View Layer, and File Output standalone preset panels now appear in the Properties tab. Three Panel classes were imported in the source but never added to the registration list, so the Output / View Layer / File Output Presets standalone panels never rendered on any render engine. The v0.7.5 fix for the Render Presets standalone panel missed three siblings; this picks them up.

  2. Blender no longer prints a flood of _RestrictContext errors at startup. Eight viewport-sync preference toggles fired their update callback during userpref restore and read the active scene before it was available, dumping a stack trace per toggle to the console on every fresh Blender launch. The callback chain now reads context state safely and exits cleanly when the scene isn't yet populated.

  3. View Layer switches are dramatically faster, even on heavy scenes. The cascade's per-switch update was generating the same naming data thousands of times and walking every object's animation targets twice — on a scene with dozens of watched objects plus a Rest Action that cost ~18 ms per switch. A series of optimisations (slot-name caching, a single merged clear-and-snap walk, and skipping objects the Rest Action doesn't cover) cut that to under 3 ms — roughly 7× faster. Undo/redo behaviour and rest-state snap-back are unchanged.

  4. Internal: codebase-audit infrastructure, dead-code cleanup, dashboard polish. The maintainer-facing /stats dashboard gained several new static-analysis checks — Restricted-Context Safety (catches the startup-spam class of bug above), Class Registration Coverage (catches classes declared but never registered), Dangling References in register/unregister, Unreachable Code, Register/Unregister Symmetry, and Orphan Functions — plus a headless Speed Benchmark now shown as its own Overview score tile. Those audits surfaced and led to the removal of ~110 unused functions and one dead module across the addon, plus the fix of a Blender + pytest + Windows interaction that was producing ~244 caught fatal exceptions per test run. No end-user behaviour change — the addon's internals are leaner and the perf/quality signal is sharper.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: 1. Rest State sub-panel under Globals. The Globals tab in the 3D View N-panel now hosts a Rest State sub-panel with the Rest Action picker and a short hint that unkeyed properties snap to the Rest Action's values.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: 1. The Rule tag's per-preset rows now expose the Storage filter and the tier badge. Each row in Globals → Rules → Preset Rules for: gains the same storage-tier dropdown that already lives on the regular preset rows, so you can aim the search popup at Personal / User / Shared / etc. before picking. A small tier icon next to the selector shows where the currently-assigned preset lives, matching the main preset row's badge.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. Three dead migration-banner buttons removed from the context panel header. The Dismiss / Migrate / Dismiss-all buttons referenced operators that did not exist anywhere in the addon — clicking any of them (if ever surfaced via the migration trigger) would raise an error on the user. The two WindowManager properties that drove the banner — never written to anywhere in the codebase — are also gone. Future migration system can re-introduce a fresh surface.
  2. Double-clicking Rebuild Cache & Reload Addon no longer raises "timer already registered". A second click within the 100 ms reload-schedule window now cancels the pending first click before scheduling a fresh one. Addon-reload failures from this flow now write to the addon's debug log instead of stdout, so the message lands somewhere users can actually find it.
  3. The keymap-audit monitor no longer ticks in the background when debug logging is off. The 2.5 s diagnostic timer still registers, but each tick now exits immediately unless the hotkeys debug log topic is actually enabled, so idle CPU stays at zero in the default end-user configuration and the console stays quiet. Turning debug logging on at runtime takes effect on the next tick — no addon reload needed.
  4. Internal: stats / quality-audit engine and dashboard upgrades. The /stats command's audit engine gained two new checks: Part R — Operator Dispatcher Audit (catches the self-recursion crash pattern that produced the New-Tag and Remove-Tag crashes in 0.7.5, plus unknown panel layout.operator() strings, missing bl_idname, duplicate bl_idname, empty execute() bodies, return None in execute, and poll() missing @classmethod — eight new sub-checks total) and Part W — Wiki Health (surfaces the wiki-drift ledger so the dashboard shows the latest known wiki state). The dashboard itself gained per-tile step controls (+1 / +2 / 5 / 10 / All / Less) on every breakdown table and bar list, a View all popup that opens a focused full-dataset modal, the file-structure tree now visible on the overview page directly, restored section drag-and-drop in layout mode, and fixes for the previously-broken excluded-checks layout and the composition card's pair-layout. The audit engine's root-path detection now auto-discovers the project root regardless of clone location (previously silently scanned 0 files when the clone wasn't at the hardcoded path). No effect on addon runtime — these changes live under doc/reports/ and the maintainer's local blender-code-quality skill, not in the shipped extension.
  5. Internal: panel-draw smoke test broadened, codebase stats refreshed. The SmartOutput panel is now actually exercised by the draw-smoke test instead of being skipped, via a synthesised compositor context. Stats engine output regenerated; scores unchanged at 10.0 / 10.0. No effect on addon runtime.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. Delete a preset file from disk with Alt+click on the X in any preset row. Plain click still clears the selection. The Alt+click path shows the standard confirmation dialog before removing the file, and is wired up across Render, Output, View Layer, File Output, Color Management, Camera, World, and Material presets.
  2. Folder icons on the Addon and Project preset locations open the folder in your file explorer. Sits in Preferences → Data → Storage, matching the Shared and Local rows. The Project icon stays disabled until the .blend is saved.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. World preset row greys out when the scene has no world assigned, with a hint pointing the user at the next step. Previously the row stayed active but picking a preset silently no-op'd because there was no world to write into.
  2. Variants list settings menu toggles index numbers and per-row stats, both off by default. Show Columns → Index Numbers swaps the leading 0, 1, 2 boxes on Product / Part / Material rows for a small dot placeholder so row alignment doesn't shift; State rows skip the dot entirely because they already lead with the active-state socket / keyframe icon; Pool-material child rows still show the actual number because it's the slot-pool position the variant cascade engine writes against. Show Columns → Stats hides the read-only count badges (state / part / material counts on Product rows, pool size and object count on Material rows). Interactive controls — the State activator, Part visibility toggles, Material pool-index field, and every Collection icon — stay visible regardless of either toggle. The old Collection toggle is removed — it was wired to a UIList that the Variants panel no longer draws.
  3. Visual gap between the Inspector's function-key icons and the view-mode toggles. The bookmarks / timeline icons now sit slightly apart from the slotted / channels toggles, so the two icon groups read as separate clusters instead of one cramped row.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. Color Management presets no longer error when applying. Curves data now round-trips cleanly through saved presets in Blender 5.1, and existing presets carrying the legacy per-curve extend field continue to load.
  2. Adding a new tag in the Tag Library no longer crashes Blender. The New → Tag button (and Ctrl+N) now creates a uniquely-named tag in the active category — or the active group when a group or grouped tag is selected — and focuses the new row for inline rename.
  3. The Render preset row is visible at the top of the Render properties tab on every render engine. Previously it was attached to a Cycles-only sub-panel and stayed hidden inside the collapsed Sampling section even there.
  4. Color Management → Working Space → File now restores correctly when you apply a saved preset. The value already round-tripped to the JSON; applying it back is now resilient to API differences between Blender 5.x point releases.
  5. Drop & Re-apply preset mode no longer fires every second. It now triggers exactly once per ViewLayer switch, matching the mode's intent. Always Sync is unchanged.
  6. Switching the Inspector to Channels view actually shows the channels. Previously the panel would enter Channels view but keep the Slotted sub-mode, so the user landed on the Scene / World / Active Object slot rows instead of the channels list. Toggling Channels on now forces the sub-mode to Channels.
  7. Removing a tag from the Tag Library no longer hard-crashes Blender. Same root cause as the New-tag crash (#2 above) — the single-tag delete dispatcher called its own bl_idname in a loop until the C stack overflowed. The operator now removes the tag from the right collection (ungrouped or grouped) and refreshes the tag tree.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. Only the active view layer's camera stays visible. A new Camera Visibility Automation under Preferences → Workflow → Automations hides every other camera and (optionally) unhides the active camera's host collection on each view-layer switch. A Reset Camera Visibility operator clears the addon's hide stamps when you want to peek.
  2. Camera Visibility master toggle in the Takes Tree Settings popover. A new row above Previews flips the automation on or off without opening Preferences, and surfaces the Reset button when active.
  3. Single-click any cascade icon to pick from a list. Plain click on Action / World / Camera / Compositor / Output Rule opens a list at the cursor with (Clear), Create New, and Rename options above. Shift+click still opens the full popover; Alt+click still clears. The camera-VL "select in viewport" gesture moves to Ctrl+Shift+click.
  4. Cascade overrides popover is now inline. The … overflow popover shows one icon per cascade prop and expands the editor inline instead of opening a second floating popup. Alt+click an inner icon clears every assignment for that prop at this level.
  5. "Never" collapse mode in Tree Design. Third option next to Dynamic and Always — cascade icons stay inline at any panel width.
  6. Right-click on a takes-tree row opens a context menu. Ctrl+RightMouse on a row in the 3D View N-panel shows the row name and a Show Overrides… entry.
  7. Workflow → Automations is grouped into collapsible categories. Ten toggles split into six boxes — Rest State, Actions, Cameras, World, Previews, Render Presets. Collapse state persists.
  8. Workflow → Sync uses the same grouped layout. Viewport Tool Settings is collapsible with the master toggle on top and the seven mirror sub-flags indented beneath. Redundant labels removed.
  9. Compositor picker popup auto-sizes with a search field. Auto-sized to the longest comp name, opens with a filter field at the top, sorted alphabetically with the active comp depressed and Clear as a footer row.
  10. N-Panel warning when the .blend was saved by a different addon version. A Cache icon appears in the warnings row on MAJOR.MINOR mismatch; clicking it expands a Rebuild Cache & Reload Addon button that wipes in-memory caches, re-stamps the scene, and reloads the addon. Targets the "already-fixed bug reappears after update" failure mode.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. Cascade icons brighten for every assignment type at every tier. Some tiers previously missed rule or preset checks and stayed dim even when an assignment existed.
  2. The … overflow chevron only brightens for icons inside the popover. Assignments on pinned-outside icons no longer light the chevron, since the pinned icon already shows its own state.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. Addon no longer writes to Blender's user keyconfig. The keyframe pies on I / Alt+I — the last holdouts after v0.7.3 — now register in the addon keyconfig only, so Blender's cold-start "rebuild user keyconfig" pass can't drop your own keymap customisations as collateral. Trade-off: an in-session Reload Addon may stop the keyframe pies firing until the next full Blender restart.
  2. Group-level Compositor "Create New" no longer crashes. Pre-existing bug reached via the new Create-from-picker entry on Scene Group rows.
  3. Compositor cascade picker actually lists the compositor node groups. The picker classes existed but were never registered, so clicking the icon used to open an empty popup.
  4. Compositor picker no longer raises on first invoke after a registration hiccup. Defensive reorder so the backing window-manager property always binds.
  5. Camera Visibility automation fires on group-tier camera assignments. Assigning a camera at VL Group / Scene Group / VL Version used to wait for the next view-layer switch before visibility updated.
  6. Cascade icons refresh instantly after a picker assignment. Picker assignments at the group tiers used to keep their stale (often red) state until the ~200 ms context tick caught up.
  7. Active takes-tree row no longer jumps after a picker assignment. The tree index is snapshotted before the assignment and restored if it drifted.
  8. Camera picker at scene-bound tiers only lists cameras in the target scene. Picking a camera from another scene used to flash in the picker and then silently auto-clear, leaving the cascade icon red with no obvious cause.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: 1. Slot bindings now survive renames across undo, file load, and action duplication. The VL switch records a stable token on every target ↔ slot pair it binds, so a future switch can rediscover the binding by identity even if the target was renamed in between — including renames that happened across an Undo / Redo / file open / Blender restart, which the existing rename detector misses because its pointer-identity cache gets flushed on those events. The token also propagates through Copy Action, Retarget Slot, and the explicit "Create Slot for Target" operators, so duplicated or retargeted actions keep their original target bindings without needing a manual relink. Tokens live in a hidden PropertyGroup registered on every animatable datablock — they never appear in the N-panel Custom Properties section, the Outliner Data API view, or any auto-drawn UI. Slot-side tokens are stored on the owning Action as a hidden {slot_handle, token} collection, because Blender 5.1's ActionSlot type accepts neither registered RNA properties nor IDProps.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. VL switch slot assignment consults the token map before falling back to name matching. Token-stamped targets bind in O(1) via the new fast path and skip the 3-candidate name chain (template label → raw target name → stale slot name). The name chain remains as the recovery path for unstamped targets, library overrides, intra-action token collisions, and any target whose token resolves to a slot of the wrong target_id_type (defensive guard against stale or cross-type tokens). Behavior for unstamped scenes is identical to 0.7.2.
  2. The slot rename healing pass under Preferences → Workflow → Automations → Auto-Rename Slots gains a complementary token-based detector. The existing pointer-identity cache continues to run; the token detector picks up renames the pointer cache misses (in-session renames after a cache flush, renames across Undo/Redo/load). Disagreements between the two detectors are logged at WARNING level and the token result wins. The pref still gates auto-apply for both paths — single toggle, single behavior.
  3. Open-folder-in-OS-explorer modifier moved from Alt+Click to Shift+Click on every Browse folder button. The five folder buttons that supported the shortcut — Sound Folder (Workflow → Sounds), Shared Presets and Local Presets (Data → Storage), Render Directory (Batch Render → Smart Output), and the Debug Console Log Files button — now respond to Shift+Click instead. Blender's upcoming PR #157872 ("Launch OS file browser when clicking on uneditable path properties") is making Alt+Click a native gesture on path fields, so our custom Alt+Click had to step aside. Plain click behavior (open Blender's file picker / log-file dropdown) is unchanged.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. Custom user keymap entries in 3D View no longer disappear after enabling the addon and restarting Blender. The Navigation pie (Ctrl+Shift+C in 3D View) and Render Scope pie (F12 in Window/EMPTY) used to bootstrap their kmi directly into wm.keyconfigs.user, which flagged the entries is_user_defined=True. On the next Blender start Blender's "rebuild user keyconfig from addon keyconfig" pass — triggered for any keymap the addon registers into — dropped every is_user_defined=True kmi in those keymaps regardless of operator, taking the user's own customisations (e.g. an action.clean rebound to Middle-Mouse, action.copy on Shift+Alt+G) with them. The Nav and Render Scope pies now register in the addon keyconfig only and rely on Blender's auto-mirror, which produces is_user_defined=False user-kc entries that aren't subject to the rebuild-wipe. The Keyframe pies on I / Alt+I keep their user-kc bootstrap because Blender 5.x's Window/EMPTY keymap won't dispatch from addon-kc-only entries — but the user's 3D View customs (the most common case) are no longer at risk.
  2. Reloading the addon from the Add-ons preferences no longer crashes Blender in rna_builtin_properties_lookup_string. The pie matcher (_find_existing_pie_kmi) and several diagnostic / UI-draw helpers (_kmi_descr, _scan_pie_entries_in_editor_keymaps, _iter_pie_kmi, _find_pie_kmi, the duplicate-operator label, the Workflow → Hotkeys row drawer, and the keymap audit snapshot helper) used to read kmi.properties.pie_id / .linked on our own tks.* operators. After a disable → enable cycle the previous session's user-kc kmis still referenced operator subtypes whose OperatorProperties SRNA had been freed during the first unregister(); the next getattr(kmi.properties, 'pie_id', '') segfaulted inside rna_builtin_properties_lookup_string → VectorSet<PropertyRNA*>. All tks.* kmi matching is now identity-only — kmi.alt distinguishes I-pie (alt=False) from Alt+I-pie (alt=True), and Shift+D (unlinked) from Alt+D (linked) on the duplicate operator. Reads on wm.call_menu_pie (Blender built-in, stable SRNA) are retained where the property name is needed as a filter.
  3. Quitting Blender with the addon enabled no longer crashes during WM_exit_ex. The same kmi.properties.<attr> reads ran on the shutdown path via diagnostic helpers and snapshot calls, hitting the same freed-SRNA segfault inside Blender's exit-time Python invocation. The identity-only matchers above remove that read on every shutdown path. The keymap-audit instrumentation that helped track down this crash (utils/keymap_audit.py) stays in the build as [AUDIT] / [AUDIT-DIFF] log lines, gated by the UI:HOTKEYS debug topic — useful for any future regression report.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. Two new opt-in keyframe pies on I and Alt+I — both off by default. The Keyframe Pie (I) covers insert/set actions; the Clear Pie (Alt+I) covers clear/remove actions. Each has its own 8-slot configurator under Preferences → Workflow → Pie Menu. Until you enable them, I and Alt+I behave exactly like stock Blender.
  2. Pie hotkeys are now rebindable. Click the center cell of any pie configurator (Navigation, F12 Render, Keyframe, Clear) and press your preferred key + modifier. A reset arrow next to it restores the built-in default.
  3. All four pie shortcuts now appear in the Hotkeys panel. Navigation, F12 Render, Keyframe, and Clear pies are listed inline under Preferences → Workflow → Hotkeys so you can rebind every pie from one place using Blender's standard keymap controls.
  4. The keyframe pies cover every keyframe-capable editor. Per-editor checkboxes under Preferences → Workflow → Pie Menu → Editor Scope opt the pies into the 3D Viewport, Properties, Graph Editor, Dope Sheet, NLA, Shader, Image, Movie Clip, Sequencer, and Outliner. Disabled editors fall back to native I / Alt+I.
  5. Hover-aware pie slots. Outside the 3D Viewport, slots that need a specific property (Set/Insert at Rest, Snap to Rest, Clear Current Keyframe, Unset Rest Key, Insert/Delete Native) only appear when the cursor is over a keyframable value. Slot directions stay reserved so muscle memory still works for the 8 directions.
  6. Three new Rest State operators: Unset Rest Key (Channel), Unset Entire Rest Slot, and Clear All Rest Keys (VL). All three are available as slot choices in the Clear Pie.
  7. Viewport tool settings now sync across scenes. A new Workflow → Sync → Viewport Tool Settings section mirrors selected viewport settings (transform, snap, proportional, mirror, auto-merge, lock-object-mode, UV) across every scene last-write-wins. Off by default — each group has its own toggle gated by a master Enable Viewport Sync switch.
  8. Auto-clearing the Scene World on an empty cascade is now optional. A new Auto-clear World when Unassigned toggle under Preferences → Workflow → Automations (on by default) controls whether scene.world gets wiped when no world is in the Takes tree. Turn it off to preserve a manually-assigned world.
  9. Apply Scene World to any cascade tier from the drift warning. The drift row for World now opens a tier-picker dropdown (Global / Scene Group / Scene / VL Group / View Layer / VL Version) — pick a tier and the active scene.world is written into that level's override. Tiers that don't apply for the active context are greyed out.
  10. Undo can no longer silently destroy a manually-assigned Scene World. A new Skip World-Clear in Post-Undo Cascade core strategy (on by default, under Debug → Undo-Redo) makes the post-undo cascade skip the world-clear branch — so undoing back to an empty cascade tree doesn't wipe scene.world.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: 1. The Inspector Watchlist keeps a stable height when it's empty. The list and its sidebar buttons (Add / Remove / Settings / Find Slot) always render now; the empty-state info appears as a row in the stats slot instead of replacing the list. The panel no longer jumps in height when the last watched object is removed.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. Pie hotkey rebinds now persist across every routine pref change. Editor Scope checkbox flips, master toggle round-trips, and addon reloads no longer wipe a custom key the user had bound via the configurator or Blender's keymap editor. Full keymap teardown only happens on addon unregister.
  2. New audit catches scope-crossing keymap writes early. The codebase stats report now flags every write to Blender's user and default keyconfigs; legitimate ones carry a justification marker and the rest deduct from the Health Score.
  3. Pie menu keymaps no longer crash Blender when you toggle Editor Scope. Re-running pie keymap registration could trigger an access violation on freed C-pointers. Teardown now matches entries by ID + properties, never dereferencing a stale pointer. Same hardening applied to Navigation, F12 Render Scope, Global Shortcuts, Tag Groups, and Context panel keymaps.
  4. The Ctrl+Shift+C navigation pie and the F12 render pie fire reliably in Blender 5.x. Both used to register only in the addon keyconfig and rely on Blender to mirror entries into the user keyconfig — which Blender 5.x doesn't do reliably. Each pie now registers in both keyconfigs explicitly.
  5. The Post-Undo Cascade Ping no longer silently aborts on scenes without a compositor node tree. A null scene.node_tree access bailed the entire ping mid-scan; the access is now guarded so the ping continues through the rest of the scan.
  6. The render-window-close watcher fires reliably during undo bursts again. The mutation-skip guard added in 0.7.1 broke the post-undo cascade ping — it relied on this timer firing promptly during undo. With the guard gone, the watcher polls normally throughout undo.
  7. The F12 Render Scope pie shows operator errors as status-bar messages instead of console tracebacks. When the inner batch render operator raises an error (e.g. "Save file before starting Batch Render!"), the pie's dispatcher now catches it and re-reports it via its own status bar.
  8. Clean Empty Slots & Actions: combined operator + preview dialog + safety pass. A single operator runs both passes after showing a preview of what will be removed. Fake-user-pinned actions and slots still referenced elsewhere are left alone.
  9. JIT rest action cleanup runs in the two merge branches that previously skipped it. Source actions left behind by two specific JIT merge paths now follow the same cleanup schedule as every other branch — no more orphan actions accumulating over long sessions.
  10. Preset capture covers Hydra Storm engine settings and the Output Time Stretching ratio. Render presets now save Hydra Storm's Export Method. Output presets now save the Time Stretching Old / New frame-mapping. Both were silently absent before.
  11. Accepting a Cascade Drift no longer crashes on World or Compositor. The drift-accept path used to write a datablock object into a string field, raising a type error. It now writes the datablock's name (and clears with "") to match the target's expected type.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: 1. Native F12 render handlers now appear in the Process Monitor. The persistent native-F12 Write and Cancel handlers are registered with the addon's Process Monitor observability layer (the same one that tracks cascade, batch render, and other long-lived handlers), so they show up in performance audits alongside the rest.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. The render-window-close watcher pauses while the addon is mid-mutation. It used to tick on every WM redraw, and during an undo / redo / apply burst the window count could read stale and trigger on_close — silently tearing down render handlers underneath an in-flight operator. The watcher now backs off for 0.3 s while a mutation is in progress, then resumes normal polling.
  2. The F12 Render Scope pie's dispatcher is now guarded against null-scene contexts. A new poll check returns false when context.scene is None (e.g. before the first .blend is loaded), so accidentally triggering the operator in that window no longer raises.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
1. Choose which actions ask before they run. The old "Protected Mode" switch is replaced by Operation Guards — pick per-area which destructive actions show a confirmation (Tree Context, Inspector, Globals, Batch Render, System, Data Integrity). One master toggle still turns the whole thing off. Preferences → Workflow → Operation Guards.
  2. Modernized Addon Preferences UI. Four tabs (Workflow, UI, Data, Debug) with their own sub-tab navigation make settings far easier to find and reduce vertical scrolling.
  3. Cleanup and Scanner utilities now share one place under Data → Tools as a high-density "Tools" sub-tab.
  4. Cleaner project configuration management. The Project tier inspector is now collapsible, has clearer icons, and tooltips explain how Local, Shared, and Project tiers relate.
  5. Tier Filter startup is now visible in the Process Monitor. Useful when diagnosing slow addon startup or registration timing.
  6. Group-deletion confirmations are now per-type. Scene Groups and View Layer Groups have separate toggles in the Operation Guards panel, so you can require confirmation for one and skip it for the other.
  7. Render Completion Sounds master toggle moved up a level. It now sits next to the other workflow options, while the three sound sub-options (Sound Folder, Success Sound, Failed Sound) stay grouped together below it.
  8. More automated UI testing. Panels, popovers, and operator tooltips now run through 5× more test cases on every build (326 vs 63), catching draw-time bugs earlier.
  9. Open folder paths in your OS file browser. Hold Alt and click any "Browse" folder button (shared presets, local presets, sound folder, directory) to open that path in Explorer / Finder / your file manager. Empty or missing paths show a status-bar message instead of an error.
  10. Render-completion sounds now fire for every render flow. Ctrl+Click on a render icon (single VL), the Render Active View Layer operator, and — optionally — Blender's native F12 / Ctrl+F12 all play the Success or Failed sound when they finish. The foreground and background batch renders already did this; the gap is now closed.
  11. Per-source sound toggles. Six new checkboxes under Preferences → Workflow → Render Completion Sounds let you decide which renders play a sound: Ctrl+Click, Foreground Batch, Background Batch, Active VL, Preview (thumbnails), and Native F12. The master Enable Render Sounds still gates all six. Preview and Native F12 are off by default to avoid surprises.
  12. Two-sub-box sound preferences layout. The audio settings (folder + Success/Failed pickers + Reset) and the per-source toggles ("Play sound for…") are now split into separate sub-boxes so the section reads cleanly at a glance.
  13. Reset to Defaults button in sound preferences. A labeled button clears the custom sound folder and resets the Success and Failed dropdowns to the bundled success_sparkle.wav / failed_buzz.wav in one click. The tiny X icon next to the folder field still works for the same purpose.
  14. 3D viewports auto-demote from Rendered to Solid during a render. Any 3D viewport set to Rendered shading is temporarily switched to Solid for the duration of a Ctrl+Click, Active VL, or batch render, then restored when the render finishes. Stops the viewport from running its own render loop in parallel and starving the offline render of GPU. Background batch renders aren't affected — they already run in a subprocess.
  15. Opt-in F12 render scope pie menu — fully configurable. A new F12 Render Pie Menu preference replaces native F12 with an 8-slot pie. Each slot is user-pickable from 10 render actions: Current View Layer, Active VLs (Scene), Active VLs (All Scenes), All VLs (Scene, Force), All VLs (All Scenes, Force), Skip Completed (resume), Failed Only (recovery pass), Preview Thumbnails, Native F12 Passthrough, and Open Render Settings. Same mutual-exclusion + reset workflow as the Ctrl+Shift+C navigation pie. Off by default — when on, F12 opens the pie; when off, F12 is fully native. Animation (Ctrl+F12) is still native — an animation pie is planned next.
  16. Batch Render sidebar buttons are now scope dropdowns. The Foreground and Background render icons in the Batch Render panel sidebar now open dropdown menus listing every render scope (Current VL, Active/Force across scopes, Skip Completed, Failed Only, Preview Thumbnails). Picking an item routes through the same dispatcher the F12 pie uses, so behavior is consistent across all render entry points. Background batch render now accepts the same scope flags as the foreground operator.
  17. Broken cascade assignments are now surfaced as their own warning panel. When a Camera, Action, World, or Compositor field at any cascade tier (Global / Scene Group / Scene / VL Group / View Layer / VL Version) points at a datablock that no longer exists, a red broken-link button appears in the navigation warning bar.
