  19. 🟢 The render queue remembers each take's recent render times together with the settings they were measured at. New persisted ViewLayer.tks_render_history StringProperty (panels/context_render_queue.py, HIDDEN, saved in the .blend like the probe estimate): JSON list, newest-first, capped at 3, entries {t: seconds, c: cost proxy at record time, e: engine, ts: unix}. Written ONLY through features/batch_render/shared.py::record_render_actual — FG render_post (render_hooks.py) and both BG done sites (process_manager.py); preview renders are excluded on every path (durations under overridden settings stamped with the real settings' cost would poison the history). Estimates use the cost-rescaled same-engine median (render_queue_eta.history_append/history_entries/estimate_from_history): changing resolution or samples rescales each stored time by the cost ratio instead of trusting it raw or discarding it (with adaptive sampling the stamp is pixels-only — entry Changed 17), cross-engine entries are skipped (a pixels×samples ratio between engines is meaningless), and the median absorbs one outlier run (thermal throttle, background load). Cascade rank 1 in _estimate_vl_render_time, with the legacy session-only tks_last_render_time float as fallback — still written for its other consumers (progress fallbacks, ops_render_queue_resolver). Tests test_render_eta_history.py. Commits cd9935d, 29daade.

  20. 🟢 Value Lock — C4D-inspired locking, rethought as a scene-wide value snap-back, toggled from the nav header. features/value_lock/ package. Semantics (FINAL, user-settled 2026-07-07 — unkeyed-only, the C4D-like lock "only for locking the global changes"): a channel WITH keyframes on the currently assigned action+action_slot is LEFT FULLY UNLOCKED (never snapped, never targeted; a nudged keyed value is re-asserted by the animation system on the next frame change, not by the lock); only UNKEYED channels snap back to their cached lock-time value (a nudged static value silently applies at every frame — the "global change" the lock prevents). engine._keyed_channels(block) is a read-only slot-exact fcurve-membership check (unresolved action_slot = not slot-driven = unkeyed; no cross-slot fallback — that over-detected on same-action VL switches) passed as keyed_now to channels_to_snap. ENFORCEMENT IS SELECTION-SCOPED (f97c970): the feeder schedules only selected/active objects (+ their data/shape keys/materials), so cascade-driven writes on unselected watchlist objects are never fought — log-confirmed root cause of the VL-switch clobbering. An a3c027e experiment (keyed→own-pose snapping via fc.evaluate) was reverted in 89bebb7 per user decision. Snap log: 'snapped N unkeyed value(s), left K keyed channel(s) free'. The feeder's Action-datablock branch was removed (keyed edits are the animator's). Autokey, however, is mutually EXCLUSIVE with the lock (de1d80a): autokey would keyframe every nudge and the unkeyed-only lock leaves keyed channels free, so autokey would let accidental edits exempt themselves. Locking forces autokey off (saving tks_value_lock_autokey_was_on), dims the header autokey button, re-asserts off if re-enabled via Blender's timeline (feeder schedules a pass → engine forces off), and restores on unlock — the v2.1 handling, kept alongside the unkeyed-only engine. Deliberate MANUAL keying still opts a channel out. lock_core.py: bpy-free decision core — capture_paths_for per rotation mode (incl. quaternion/axis-angle), channels_to_snap (EPS idempotency), merge_targets (keyed targets override baseline), prune_dangling, locked_names_in_updates; 20 unit tests in dev/tests/test_value_lock.py. properties.py: .blend-backed store — Scene.tks_value_lock_enabled Bool (flipped ONLY by the toggle operator so flag+capture pair in one undo step), Scene.tks_value_lock_autokey_was_on, and Scene.tks_value_locks CollectionProperty of TKS_ValueLockEntry {id_name, data_path, array_index, baseline}, IDs keyed by NAME string per the PointerProperty ban; lock_all(scene) captures every scene.objects member. engine.py: debounced snap-back (0.1s timer + 0.08s drag-defer, mutation-queue routed, undo/redo + is_snap_suppressed guards). handler.py: read-only depsgraph feeder — cheapest-first enabled gate, autokey-mirror gate skeleton (50ms throttle, Process Monitor "Value Lock", playback/addon_is_mutating/batch skips); reacts to object is_updated_transform hits AND bpy.types.Action datablock updates (covers key deletes turning a channel unkeyed). Rest-state integration: mirror_logic._handle_autokey_mirror and revert_snap._revert_unkeyed_to_rest_deferred stand down while the lock is on (the lock owns the scene — no rest-vs-lock tug-of-war; see doc/troubleshooting/value_lock_cascade_action_fight.md for the v2 cascade-corruption bug this replaced). Autokey integration: engaging the lock remembers autokey and forces it off (a lock prevents keying entirely); unlock restores; the header autokey button dims while locked; feeder+engine force autokey back off if re-enabled mid-lock. operators.py: tks.value_lock_toggle is the single global toggle. UI: the nav-header button between Autokey and Timeline Sync (panels/navigation/panel.py, LOCKED/UNLOCKED + depress) is the SINGLE control (the earlier Inspector status panel was removed). Materials (ce2ff49): the store is generalized to typed (id_type, name) tokens — OBJECT transform channels + MAT node-tree sockets. lock_all also captures every numeric node-socket (inputs AND outputs — Value/RGB nodes hold data on outputs) of every material used by scene objects (add_material_lock, tree-relative nodes["X"].inputs[i].default_value paths that match the tree's fcurve data_paths); resolve_token maps MAT→material.node_tree (values + animation_data owner); the feeder schedules all locked materials on any Material/NodeTree depsgraph update (tree names don't map back to materials cheaply; engine only writes drift); scalar socket writes route through the parent (path_resolve(parts[0]) + setattr). Snap-on-release (ce2ff49): the debounced flush defers while any TRANSFORM_OT_* modal operator is running (Window.modal_operators, verified in 5.1), so the snap happens on mouse release, never mid-grab; slider drags keep deferring via the 0.08s value-change window. Everything-animatable (d07981e + 5532755): capture is now the generic RNA rule — every writable numeric property Blender marks is_animatable (_capture_rna_animatable), over: object props, pose-bone transforms (armatures), modifier RNA props + Geometry Nodes group inputs (ID properties; guarded — most modifier types raise TypeError on .keys()), constraint props, the object's data block, shape-key values, material props, and the scene world (props + node tree). Token kinds grew to OBJECT/DATA/SHAPEKEY/MAT/MATP/WORLD/WORLDNT, each resolving to the block that owns both values and fcurves so the keyed/unkeyed rules apply uniformly; DATA/SHAPEKEY are keyed by the OBJECT name so data renames don't orphan them. The Scene datablock and compositor trees are deliberately excluded — the cascade/render-preset system writes those and the lock must not fight the addon. Engine scales via one-pass grouped_baselines (store holds thousands of entries now) and _write_channel gains int/bool/ID-property write casts. VL-switch + lag fixes (3a2e0fa, corrected by 2d9cdaf): baselines are FROZEN at lock time and never re-based — unkeyed channels always return to their locked value across VL switches, keyed channels follow whatever action is now assigned (fc.evaluate, switch-aware); the only switch handling is a non-sticky stand-down while mutation_queue.is_in_vl_switch_recovery() (new read-only accessor) is open, so enforcement never reads a half-applied action mid-swap. (3a2e0fa first tried re-basing to the arrived-at state via a refresh_baselines poller armed by note_vl_switch; that was racy — it dropped the pending snap and froze an un-snapped user move as the new baseline, and its _awaiting_refresh flag could stick true and silently disable the lock. Removed in 2d9cdaf per user decision.) UI lag killed: the feeder's FOUR full store scans per tick became one cached derived view (token_sets+grouped_baselines, validated by scene/count/enabled, invalidated on refresh and undo-abort). Repro + fix proven by smoke 4i/4j (fails pre-fix). See doc/troubleshooting/value_lock_vl_switch_stale_baseline.md. Known scope: IDs added after locking aren't captured; renamed objects/materials drop out via prune; NLA untouched. Design record doc/roadmap/_dev/value-lock.md. Suite: 865 passed, no new failures; 32/32 live-extension smoke (unkeyed→cached start, keyed→own keyed value, cascade action swap persists untouched, material socket snap unkeyed+keyed, light energy, modifier float, shape key, world background, boolean-cast prop, autokey pause/restore/re-enable guard). Commits 7aa693e, aada3cc, f33792d, 96d030a, c015772, ce2ff49, d07981e, 5532755, 3a2e0fa, 2d9cdaf. VERIFIED in-app 2026-07-08 after a stabilization chain: engine-owned switch clock fed by VL_SWITCH_PRE/POST_CASCADE (f72cfc8+c281e39 — mutation_queue's elastic recovery window is re-marked by preload/slot ops and stretched settling to 20s+), feeder drop-during-1s-settle (chain writes to selected objects must not be enforced), explicit post-switch keyed restore via fc.evaluate writes (frame_set proved a no-op interactively), DRIVEN channels classified animation-owned (25891c7 — snapping a driven value fights the driver forever), and the final root cause of the recurring "same-action switch" reports fixed in the SWITCH PIPELINE itself, not the lock (Fixed entry 17 / ccac6d1).

  21. 🟢 Rest State Mode — read-time ACTION-tier override toggled from the nav header. The take tree is NEVER written: resolve_cascade() (core/cascade.py) gains a raw: bool = False param and a top-of-function override — while Scene.tks_rest_mode (master-scene flag, rest-action-name persistence pattern) is on, CascadeProperty.ACTION resolves to the rest action with source label "Rest Mode"; mode off / no rest action / accessor failure all fall through to real tree resolution (exception-proofed seam _rest_mode_action_name, monkeypatchable for pure tests). Choke-point placement makes every consumer consistent by construction — including the post-undo cascade refire (vl_lifecycle re-resolves, so undo across the toggle restores flag+assignments together). raw=True consumers that must never see the mode: batch_pipeline.py + cli_worker.py (the worker opens .blends where the scene-persisted flag may be saved ON) and the naming watchdog _auto_rename_cascade_actions (name expectations must never target the rest action — rest_action_empty_after_reference_rename.md). Standdowns while on: _get_slot_mismatches (panels/navigation/logic.py, head guard) and get_rest_drifts (rest_state/logic.py) return [] — their Revert/Snap buttons would fight the mode. features/rest_state/mode.py: flag + accessors (is_rest_mode via getattr — id.get() is blind to bpy.props), pause_autokey/restore_autokey (all scenes, per-scene tks_rest_mode_autokey_was_on), conflicting_value_lock, find_slot_collisions, and tks.rest_mode_toggle (REGISTER+UNDO): Value Lock mutual exclusion BOTH directions (each toggle releases the other with an INFO report), instant apply via resolve + apply_vl_switch_unified on the active VL (no layer switch needed), force_sync_inspector + signal_redraw. UI: ghost button (GHOST_ENABLED/DISABLED + depress) between the lock and Timeline Sync; autokey dim condition extended to the mode; the Context-panel ghost (draw_tree.py/draw_split.py) depresses + alerts in-mode as the viewport badge; tooltip in texts/tooltips_rest_state.py. Collision guard (spike finding A — label-based binding can collapse targets onto one rest slot and cross-bleed): the unified sweep counts per-slot bindings when its target IS the rest action (zero cost otherwise) and force-logs a WARNING listing shared slots. Mechanics de-risked by dev/experiments/rest_mode_spike.py (27/27: per-object slot binding incl. Empty divergence, uncaptured objects HOLD pose, mode-off exact restore, rest action unmutated, idempotent); spike finding B: headless tests must import the REGISTERED namespace (bl_ext.user_default...) — repo-path imports break prefs-dependent naming (New_* label collapse). 20 tests: dev/tests/test_rest_mode.py (pure seam + standdown sentinels + raw-bypass conformance) and test_rest_mode_sweep.py (registered end-to-end through the operator; fixture assigns the take via vl.tks_action — mode-off on a cascade-less layer unassigns exactly like a real VL switch). Known scope: manual (non-tree) action assignments are unassigned by the mode-off sweep, matching existing VL-switch behavior. Design record doc/roadmap/_dev/rest-state-mode.md; plan dev/plan_rest_state_mode.md. Suite from main: 34/34 targeted gate; worktree full suite 885 passed (19 known order-dependent preset fails, auditor-engine errors are worktree-path only). Commits 83dc55a, 9917bc8, 9ae77b1, 525d1a2, c088e5b.

  22. 🟢 A GPU banner in the 3D viewport surfaces any running addon process, so background work isn't mistaken for a freeze — the addon's first blf/gpu draw handler. Two new modules plus a general "active process" source. utils/process_activity.py (pure, no module-level bpy; unit-tested headless): the single source the overlay reads — a PUSH registry (publish(key,label,progress)/clear/clear_all, insertion-ordered Activity(key,label,progress) namedtuples) plus PULL providers (register_provider(fn), fn(context)→iterable of (key,label[,progress])). snapshot(context) merges push-first then providers in registration order, first-key-wins dedup, skips falsy labels, and isolates each provider in try/except so one blowing up never sinks the snapshot. Deliberately SEPARATE from process_monitor (which tracks registered handlers/timers, permanent while enabled) — this tracks transient work with a user-facing label. panels/process_overlay.py: PURE core (clamp01, progress_text None→''/0.5→'50%'/clamped, truncate_label ellipsis, should_show, build_lines→OverlayLine(label,pct,progress); unit-tested) + BPY engine — a SpaceView3D.draw_handler_add(_draw_callback,(),'WINDOW','POST_PIXEL') banner (blend ALPHA→NONE in finally; gpu.shader.from_builtin('UNIFORM_COLOR') filled rects; blf.size(0,px) text; per-row progress bar; ui_scale-aware; centered/cornered per pref). Crash-safety (Constitution #2/#4): handle stored module-global, added at register() / removed at unregister(); callback is READ-ONLY and wrapped in try/except; refresh piggybacks on existing signal_redraw() calls (the preload tick + batch handlers already fire them) — never area.tag_redraw(); tracked via process_monitor.register_process("Process Overlay", HANDLER, UI_SYNC, feature_group="ui_sync"). Two built-in pull providers registered in process_overlay.register() (_PROVIDERS): _preload_provider reads core.vl_preload.is_preloading()/preload_progress() (stateless — self-clears with the run, no publish/clear plumbing in the preload tick); _batch_provider reads panels/render_queue_panel._get_batch_progress(wm) gated on stats['active'] (in-flight only, not the results pane), label = status_label, bar = overall. Push API stays available for future ad-hoc processes. Prefs: new TKS_OverlayPropsMixin (preferences/props/props_overlay.py, added to _MIXINS in addon_prefs.py) in a new Interface > Overlay subtab (active_subtab_ui gained OVERLAY, drawn in draw_interface.py) under a "3D Viewport" accordion (expand_overlay_viewport, wired into ACCORDION_GROUPS + prefs_search.SEARCH_INDEX) — overlay_position EnumProperty with 6 anchors (TOP/BOTTOM × LEFT/CENTER/RIGHT, TOP_CENTER default) + OFF master kill-switch, overlay_offset_x/overlay_offset_y IntProperty (PIXEL, ±2000) applied ui-scaled from the anchor by pure anchor_position() (+x right, +y up), overlay_opacity FloatProperty (FACTOR 0–1, default 1.0) scaling every alpha via pure apply_opacity(), + overlay_hide_when_panel_open BoolProperty (default False); EXPECTED_PROP_COUNT 506→514 across this feature. Drawn in preferences/draw_workflow.py (search_prop + sub-flags shown only when overlay_position != 'OFF'). Hide-when-open reads the drawn viewport's region.active_panel_category == "Takes" (+ space.show_region_ui), so it suppresses per-viewport where the Takes N-panel is showing. Registered as panels.process_overlay in panels/__init__.py; reload block gained utils.process_activity + panels.process_overlay. Mode badges (status-HUD layer). Beyond progress rows, the banner surfaces persistent on/off MODES as colored pills at the top. Kept in process_overlay.py (not process_activity — modes are a visual overlay concept, not transient work): pure select_active_modes(candidates) filters ModeCandidate(key,label,color,active,alert) → ModeBadge (unit-tested, 5 cases); bpy collect_modes(context) reads the mode flags read-only — Autokey scene.tool_settings.use_keyframe_insert_auto (red), Value Lock scene.tks_value_lock_enabled (amber), Rest Mode rest_state.mode.is_rest_mode() (teal; rest_mode_broken() → alert red + " ⚠" label), Still Mode still_mode.engine.is_still_global() (purple), Timeline Sync wm.tks_timeline_sync (indigo) — each gated by a per-mode show toggle (overlay_mode_autokey/_value_lock/_rest/_still/_timeline_sync, default True; the AND of on-state and toggle folds into ModeCandidate.active) so the user picks which pills appear (icon-toggle row in the prefs). +5 props → EXPECTED_PROP_COUNT 514→519. _render_banner(context, mode_badges, lines, position) draws each pill as an accent-tinted chip (0.35 alpha) + a brightened accent dot (pure lighten() toward white — a same-hue dot on a same-hue tint had too little contrast for medium hues like Timeline Sync blue) + label above the progress rows, sizing the banner to the wider of the two. Two new prefs on TKS_OverlayPropsMixin: overlay_show_modes (default True — modes master) + overlay_modes_hide_when_panel_open (default False), so mode pills have their OWN panel-open behavior distinct from the progress rows (overlay_hide_when_panel_open, relabelled "Hide Progress when Takes Panel Open"). _draw_callback now gates progress and modes separately against _takes_panel_visible. Layout + draw helpers (pure, tested): banner_width() scopes the 200px min-width floor to the progress-row block only, so a modes-only banner shrinks tight to its pills (no empty gap); anchor_position() maps the 6-anchor enum + pixel offsets to the banner's bottom-left; round_rect_geometry() builds a center-fan-triangulated rounded rectangle (radius clamped to half the box; a plain quad at radius 0) that _draw_round_rect renders — the banner (~8px), pills (~6px) and mode dots (circles) now have soft corners while thin bars/accents stay square; apply_opacity() scales any RGBA's alpha by the master factor; lighten() mixes toward white for the dot. Tests: dev/tests/test_process_activity.py (16 — registry/provider/dedup/crash-isolation) + dev/tests/test_process_overlay.py (51 — pure format/decision core, mode selection, banner_width, anchor_position, round_rect geometry, apply_opacity, lighten); GPU drawing (progress bars + mode pills) verified in-app, not unit-tested (per the preload-engine convention). Full suite 1151 passed, 1 skipped.

  23. 🟢 Still & Animation Mode — pin a take's timeline to frame 0. New self-contained feature features/still_mode/ (mirrors rest_state / value_lock); core touched by only a one-line hook. engine.py (context-free, lazy bpy, unit-testable without Blender): clamp_scene(scene, use_frame_set=True) snapshots the scene's real frame_start/end/current + frame_preview_* / use_preview_range into .blend-persisted Scene.tks_still_saved_* props, sets tks_still_active=True, pins frame_start=frame_end=0 (use_preview_range=False) and jumps to 0; restore_scene reverses it verbatim; the tks_still_active guard is the double-capture guard (never re-snapshots the pinned 0→0 over the real range on a Still→Still switch). apply_still_for_vl(scene, view_layer, global_still=None, use_frame_set=True) clamps or restores off the want-rule scene_wants_still(vl, global) = is_still_global() or is_still(vl), so the global master switch and the per-take flag compose automatically (a per-take Still take stays clamped after the global switch goes off). _set_frame uses Scene.frame_set from operator context but a plain frame_current= assignment when use_frame_set=False — the switch-handler path, where frame_set inside depsgraph_update_post recurses/crashes. properties.py: Scene.tks_still_mode on the master scene (global switch, Rest State first-scene pattern), ViewLayer.tks_still (per-take, self-registered like tks_batch_selected), Scene.tks_still_active + tks_still_saved_{start,end,current,preview_start,preview_end,use_preview}. operators.py: tks.still_global_toggle (flips the master flag → apply_still_all_scenes, which re-applies the shared engine to every scene) and tks.still_toggle (scene_name/vl_name args per Rule 71.248, flips vl.tks_still, applies immediately only when the target is the active take of the visible scene). Hook: core/vl_switch.py::_handle_vl_switch_impl calls apply_still_for_vl(scene, view_layer, use_frame_set=False) right after apply_vl_switch_unified, riding the switch's mark_vl_switch_recovery grace window so the saved-range write folds into the switch's own undo step. Render scoping: features/batch_render/batch_pipeline.py::apply_render_overrides (SHARED by the FG modal pipeline AND the headless CLI worker, so one hook covers both) calls apply_still_for_vl(sc, vl, use_frame_set=True) — a Still take renders frame 0, same-scene animation takes restore via the persisted saved frame, and the CLI worker never saves the .blend so the clamp can't leak to the file. Autokey deliberately NOT paused (the 0→0 clamp already forces keys onto frame 0); NO Value-Lock / Rest-State mutual exclusion (orthogonal axes — frame vs value vs action). UI: global toggle in panels/navigation/panel.py (RENDER_STILL / RENDER_ANIMATION, beside Timeline Sync) + per-take toggle/badge on the take row in panels/context/draw_layers.py (.active-dimmed film icon). Registered via features/__init__.py _features + the __init__.py reload block. Tests: dev/tests/test_still_mode.py (12 — clamp/restore round-trip incl. preview range, double-capture guard, want-rule, global-vs-per-VL compose, scene isolation, handler-path-never-calls-frame_set, live icon-enum validity). Full suite 996 passed (0 new failures vs the main baseline); registration smoke green (props + both operators + a live clamp/restore round-trip on a real scene). Plan dev/plan_still_animation_mode.md, build sheet doc/roadmap/_dev/still-animation-mode.md. Timeline Sync (the "Frame Sync" frame_change_post handler that copies frame_current to every scene) is now mutually exclusive with global Still Mode (it fights the frame-0 pin). tks.still_global_toggle remembers wm.tks_timeline_sync in a new session WM prop tks_still_sync_was_on, forces it off when Still turns on (fires _on_timeline_sync_changed → unregisters the handler; button un-lit + greyed via row.enabled = not is_still_global(); the overlay pill drops), and restores the prior value when Still turns off. _on_frame_change_sync also early-returns on is_still_global() as a belt-and-suspenders behavior gate. Net: the button no longer reads as an active state while Still holds, and the sync is genuinely inactive. Nav-header button presentation (2026-07-12, 🟢 confirmed in-app): the global toggle now uses onion-skin state icons (_gicon = PREVIEW_RANGE off / ONIONSKIN_OFF Still / ONIONSKIN_ON Animate) and opens a drop-down of Still / Animation / Off — a new registered bpy.types.Menu TKS_MT_StillGlobal in features/still_mode/menus.py (own register/unregister, wired into the feature __init__.py; kept a separate file to avoid an operator+menu R-MIXED-ARCHETYPES mix). The button stays a layout.operator(..., depress=bool(_gmode)) (so it lights blue while a mode is set and preserves the exact invoke/execute path the viewport Still-Mode pill reads via is_still_global()); its invoke opens the menu with bpy.ops.wm.call_menu('INVOKE_DEFAULT', name="TKS_MT_StillGlobal") — a title-less menu that drops from the button like the tree "+" add menu, replacing the earlier titled wm.popup_menu closure. A first attempt using layout.menu for the button itself regressed both the blue state and the pill (a layout.menu button can't depress); see doc/troubleshooting/still_mode_button_dropdown_lost_blue_and_overlay.md. Commits 2a9b34a, 024a03c. Per-take Animate ranges + Timeline Options popover + pinned default frame (2026-07-13, 🟢 confirmed in-app): the per-tier control (draw_still_button, drawn at all 6 cascade rows) resolves to a full override cascade — tri-state tks_still ('' inherit / 'STILL' / 'ANIM') at every tier via get_cascade_winner('tks_still', …, global_prop_name='tks_global_still'), is_still_global()/is_anim_global() master short-circuits. TKS_OT_StillPopover.invoke: plain click → quick mode popup_menu ("(Clear)" icon X + separator + Still/Animate, camera-picker style); Shift-click → invoke_popup (buttonless, live) — needs execute() present or invoke_popup silently no-ops (that was the "no popover" bug, NOT the UIList); Alt-click → clear. The popover draws house label|control split rows (_labeled_row, factor 0.32): Mode = a real dropdown via a mode_enum EnumProperty(get=_mode_get, set=_mode_set) proxy over the tier's tks_still (stays open on pick; the setter routes through the shared _apply_tier, never bpy.ops from a setter); Frame = tks_anim_default_frame + tks_anim_pin (pin icon), grayed while Still (Still is always frame 0); Range = tks_anim_start (clock icon TIME) / tks_anim_end, bright only while the take's own value is ANIM. Notices via ui.styles.wrapped_info_box(kind='tip') (header + ✕), wording in texts/infoboxes.py, one line so the 300px popup can't clip. Engine: resolve_still_target returns (0,0) Still / (start,end) when the take itself wins ANIM / None; apply_switch_snap(scene, vl) jumps the playhead to tks_anim_default_frame on the interactive VL-switch seam ONLY (core/vl_switch.py + operators/context/ops_crud_switch.py, never batch_render → no render effect), clamped into the scene range and skipped when _timeline_sync_on(). _apply_tier shared by tks.still_set + the mode-enum setter. Range/mode edits persist across a Still↔Animate round-trip; only Clear (value='', the Default item + Alt-click) resets range + pin. Crash saga (see still_mode_playback_cancel_crash.md): stopping playback on a single-frame Still via screen.animation_cancel from a timer hard-crashes (ED_screen_animation_play null-deref @0x28) — needs a temp_override(window,screen) AND an is_scrubbing guard (scrubbing counts as is_animation_playing with NULL animtimer); nested submodules don't reload on disable/enable (full restart). Non-active-take timeline bug (fixed 1e563f6, still_mode_nonactive_take_clamps_active.md): _apply_tier force-applied the clicked take's clamp to its scene, moving the active take's shared timeline; now always _reapply (apply_still_all_scenes → resolve each scene for its ACTIVE take), and _on_anim_range_update clamps only when the edited VL is the scene's active take. New props: ViewLayer.tks_anim_start/end/default_frame/pin. Tests: test_still_mode.py grew to 31 (persistence round-trip, non-active-no-clamp, switch-snap pin/sync/clamp, single-frame detection, icon-enum validity). Commits a4aa2e7·7012b24·b0c035c·9e81070·6981f9c·a8c0723·3f947f4·0abc759·ae038af·6ba02af·1e563f6.

  24. 🟢 A Pacing setting (Gentle / Balanced / Fast) chooses how hard the view-layer preloader rests between layer builds. A build occupies the UI thread for its whole duration (a depsgraph build is atomic in Blender — it cannot be sliced or backgrounded), so pacing trades session smoothness against total preload time. New preload_pacing EnumProperty in TKS_AutomationsPropsMixin (default BALANCED), drawn indented under the preload mode for any enabled mode in preferences/draw_workflow.py (prefs-search aware via search_prop/rf); EXPECTED_PROP_COUNT 519→520. Pure PACING table in core/vl_preload.py: GENTLE/BALANCED/FAST = rest gap 4×/3×/2× the last build's time, floors 0.5/0.3/0.3 s, caps 5/3/2 s; next_tick_interval(exec, pacing) clamps (unknown tier → FAST). TDD test_next_tick_interval_pacing_tiers. Commit 9bb84f6.

  25. 🟢 Hotkey-openable Icon Sheet with a Recent tab. New tks.view_icon_sheet — a browse-only invoke_popup that reuses the category picker's body, factored out of TKS_OT_PickCategoryIcon into module-level _draw_icon_sheet_body / _draw_icon_subgroups / _draw_icon_grid (cell-factory parameterized) in features/smart_syntax/registry.py. Cells copy the icon id via tks.copy_icon_name (wm.clipboard), drawn emboss=False to match the picker's flat grid. Opened from an addon-keyconfig Window/EMPTY keymap registered UNASSIGNED (type='NONE') so it can't collide out of the box (per no-user-kc-bootstrap; identity-only teardown per no-rna-in-unregister); a rebind row + an "Open Icon Sheet" button live in the renamed Developer → Utilities sub-tab (preferences/draw_debug.py::draw_debug_misc, search-exposed via SEARCH_EXTRA['DEBUG']['MISC']). "Recent" tab: synthetic __tks_recent__ group pinned first in the shared side-menu enum (_icon_group_enum_items), backed by a new capped, most-recent-first custom_token_recent_icons JSON-array pref (TKS_CustomTokensPropsMixin; EXPECTED_PROP_COUNT 521→522), fed by both tks.copy_icon_name and tks.custom_token_set_category_icon. Pure helpers recent_icons_from / push_recent_icon_in (dedup + cap 33, corrupt-safe) in custom_tokens.py. The browse sheet opens on Recent when history exists (else the first category); the picker keeps opening on the first category. Tests dev/tests/test_icon_sheet_hotkey.py (9 — registration, unassigned keymap, Developer label, Misc subtab, searchable, recency helpers, copy-records-recent, Recent-pinned-first); full suite 1190 passed. Commits 6fcece7, 9901a7c, 57de8fe, 3ea0dca.

  26. 🟢 The switch profiler shows a Camera Visibility row. apply_overrides() times the apply_camera_visibility call on full switches (prop_filter is None) and records (fired, ms, camera_count) in module-level _camera_vis_last (plain data — WM and undo untouched), exposed via get_camera_vis_telemetry(). panels/navigation/profiler_feed.py::rebuild_profiler_tree adds a "Camera Visibility" row under Apply Static Overrides, gated on PHASE_OVERRIDES in ran so sweep-only / undo rebuilds show SKIPPED instead of stale telemetry — SUCCESS with time + camera count when it fired, SKIPPED ("automation off") otherwise. Full-switch-only recording so a picker / preload CAMERA-only refire never clobbers the switch reading. Confirmed in-app (row present + correct SKIPPED state with the automation off). Commit 8aa4838.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 The View-Layer Preload panel now behaves like the other navigation warning panels.
  2. Mutual exclusivity: added tks_show_preload_panel to _warn_props in panels/navigation/__init__.py, so the shared _make_warning_callback closes the others when it opens (and vice versa) instead of stacking on top.
  3. Total bar relocated to the nav header: panels/navigation/panel.py — the thin batch-render progress slot (_bar_row in nav_col, text=" ", no numbers) now also renders the preload total (is_preloading() → preload_progress() factor) when no batch render is occupying it. The in-panel total bar in warnings/preload_viewlayers.py was removed (its preload_progress import dropped); the ETA row stays.
  4. 🟢 "Snap Active to Rest" is grayed out in the keyframe pie when the active object has nothing to snap back to. _active_lacks_rest_slot(context) in panels/pie_menus/keyframe_pies.py checks that a Rest Action exists but the active object has no matching rest slot; the SNAP_TO_REST slot is then drawn disabled (grayed, still visible) rather than hidden — so the user sees it exists and why it is unavailable (c389f33, superseding the earlier hide-via-pie.separator() approach). The operator tks.snap_prop_to_rest's poll is unchanged, so the rest-drift panel's per-property revert buttons (non-active targets) are unaffected.

  5. 🟢 Internal: annotated Takes code with auditor-awareness markers for intentional whole-file data scans and display-only icon maps — no behavior change. ✅ VERIFIED: auditor respects both sentinels; PERF-03 violations 121→0, CORR-08 violations 1→0. Annotations only (comments), clearing the remaining PERF-03 / CORR-08 auditor findings on Takes after the auditor's own over-broad checks were fixed (see blender-addon-auditor REG-07/PERF-03/MAINT-02/CORR-08 false-positive fixes).

  6. 🟢 Hovering a token in the Build Syntax popover shows what it is and what it resolves to right now. New token_builder.token_tooltip(raw, context) returns the registry description + live value as two lines ("Scene Group name\nNow: Ep01"), replacing TKS_OT_BuilderAppend.description's value-only "Preview: …" string — so look-alike tokens ({group} "Node group/parent" vs {scenegroup} "Scene Group name" vs {vlgroup} "VL Group name") can be told apart on hover. Falls back to the bare {token} when no description is registered, and to (not set in this context) when nothing resolves (no fake value). Reuses token_example. Tests test_token_tooltip_*. Commit b47373b.

  7. 🟢 Internal: the Build Syntax popover and the node-editor token panel now share one token grid — no behavior change (grouping only). New token_builder.TOKEN_CATEGORY_MODEL — one (key, title, icon, [bare names]) list, a superset of every non-separator UNIFIED_TOKENS token — plus categorized_tokens(filter_kind, filter_key, *, search='') (context + search filtered via the existing tokens_for_filter/match_token, empty categories dropped, grouped by _TOKEN_CATEGORY_INDEX). Both surfaces consume it: panel._draw_builder_columns (CONTEXT/RENDER_PATH, append, dim, value-toggle) and panel._draw_cheatsheet_accordion (same, copy, WM expand keys _tks_cheat_expand_<key>). Separators (sep/sep1..5) and path literals (//,../) are deliberately NOT in the model — each shell draws its own bespoke separator/path block. TOKEN_ALIASES={'viewlayer'}; a superset-coverage test asserts every UNIFIED_TOKENS and TOKENS_PER_TEMPLATE token is categorized so none can silently vanish. Cosmetic category-boundary shifts (e.g. blend now under File & System; popover gains {frame_start}/{frame_end}/{variant_index}). 11 new tests; full suite 583 passed. Commit 5ae792c. (The 'NAMING'/'TEMPLATE' filter kinds were added here for the Preferences token browser, which has since been removed — see this release's Removed section; the kinds stay in token_builder for completeness.)

  8. 🟢 The naming separator character moved to a new Pie & Misc > Tokens section. With the Tokens sub-tab gone (see Removed), naming_separator — still read by the naming engine as what {sep} resolves to — kept its only editor. Relocated the single editable row (right-aligned "Separator" label + prop(self, "naming_separator") + a tks.copy_token {sep} clipboard button) into a new "Tokens" collapsible at the end of draw_hotkeys_pie (preferences/ui_draw_hotkeys.py), gated by a new expand_pie_tokens BoolProp (props/props_logging.py, default False) added to the Pie & Misc ACCORDION_GROUPS tuple. The read-only fixed sep1-5 / path-literal list was not carried over (those are reference, already shown beside the tokens in the mega-sheet). EXPECTED_PROP_COUNT net 496→481.

  9. 🟢 Internal: the Smart Output token engine was extracted into a standalone features/smart_syntax/ feature — no behavior change. Moved engine.py (SmartSyntaxEngine, UNIFIED_TOKENS, BRACKET_STYLES), engine_token_resolvers.py→resolvers.py (the 7 resolver clusters + ABSENCE_SENTINELS), and token_builder.py→builder.py (pure pattern logic + the tks.token_builder_* operators + shared draw) out of features/batch_render/ into new features/smart_syntax/. Split the 12 WindowManager.tks_token_* runtime props into smart_syntax/properties.py, the keep-open TKS_PT_TokenBuilderPopup + draw_smart_syntax_cheat_sheet into smart_syntax/panel.py, and the token ops TKS_OT_CopySyntax / TKS_OT_SyntaxCheatSheetPopover into smart_syntax/operators.py. batch_render is now a consumer: its TKS_PT_SmartOutput node panel + the directory/filename field ops (TKS_OT_BrowseDirectory/ResetDirectory/ToggleOutputMode) stay and import the engine/builder via ..smart_syntax. The feature registers before batch_render in features/__init__ (and the root __init__ reload block) since both consume its engine + WM state; register() order is properties → builder → operators → panel (engine/resolvers are pure, classless). All bl_idnames are unchanged — no operator renames, so no UI/keymap references needed touching. TOKENS_PER_TEMPLATE deliberately stays in preferences/naming.py (deviation from the original plan's templates.py): it's consumed at module-load by naming.py (_FIELD_ACTION_PANELS) and preferences/__init__, and preferences imports before features (root __init__ line ~298 vs ~304), so a module-level smart_syntax.templates import would force the whole features package to load mid-preferences-import — a circular-import risk; builder.py reads TOKENS_PER_TEMPLATE lazily as before. Re-routed every engine importer (batch_render/{panel,handlers,batch_pipeline,ops_utils,ops_smart_output}.py, operators/misc/ops_render_queue_resolver.py, preferences/naming.py) plus dev/tests/test_token_builder.py. builder.py's lazy _get_compositor_trees import now points at ..batch_render.handlers (deferred, no module-level cycle). No persisted datablock property, no _SAVEABLE_PREFS entry, no save-file/undo impact — pure relocation + import rewiring. Live drift: median actual/estimated ratio over THIS run's DONE takes (collect_drift_ratios/drift_factor, clamped 0.25–4.0; DONE-only — FAILED/CANCELLED durations are truncated) rescales the remaining frozen-snapshot estimates for the ETA text; the bar keeps reading the snapshot so its monotonicity is untouched. Live re-guard (reguard_estimate): a snapshot row disagreeing with the scene's measured reality median by more than STALE_RATIO=2.0 either way — and lacking own measurement — snaps to the sibling median mid-batch (re-guarded rows skip drift; rows with real history are never clamped, so a legitimately-10x hero take survives). Sibling reality via _vl_reality_seconds: session actual OR persisted history — the SKIP_SAVE tks_last_render_time starts every session at 0, which had left the guard blind after each restart. In-flight remainder priority: engine-native Remaining (entry 18) → progress projection elapsed×(1−p)/p blended by p (project_in_flight_remaining; the realness flags TKS_OT_BatchRender._has_real_progress / manager _has_real_progress are authoritative — tks_render_progress also carries the elapsed-based fallback, which would be circular) → estimate − elapsed. Displayed total = recorded actuals for DONE + corrected estimates for the rest. Throttled [BATCH:ETA] diagnostic trace (gated enable_debug_logging + log_topic_batch). FG limitation settled: Blender 5.x render_stats delivers only the final Time:…(Saving:…) string (log-verified + devtalk #220) — no per-sample FG observer exists, so projection is BG-only and the FG Sample X/Y hook stays dormant.   19. 🟢 Calibrate Render Times probes each take at a reduced resolution under the real sampling settings. Root cause of the estimate blow-ups: the old worker probed at forced 64²×1-sample / 256²×16-sample points and extrapolated on pixels × nominal samples with a ~100× lever arm — structurally wrong under adaptive sampling (Cycles terminates per pixel at the noise threshold and never nears the nominal cap), and one-off warm-up cost (kernel compile / BVH build) landing in a probe timing multiplied by the lever. Failed intermediate the same day: warm-up + denoise/compositing post-probe + monotonic floor on the two-axis model — reduced the noise but not the structural adaptive error. Final cli_probe_worker.py design (render-farm still-image test strategy rethought for per-VL probing): untimed warm-up, then two probes at 12.5% / 25% of the real output resolution (floor 64px/axis) with samples, adaptive sampling, denoising AND compositing left at scene values; _extrapolate_resolution fits overhead+slope on the pixel axis alone, floored at time_high. Adaptive-aware cost model: use_adaptive_sampling → pixels-only cost in BOTH _vl_cost_proxy and shared.record_render_actual (the two must agree or history rescaling drifts). Probe sanity guard in render_queue_eta.cascade_estimate: a probe disagreeing with the measured sibling median by >2× either way is distrusted → sibling median (still trusted when no sibling reality exists). Fingerprint probe-sharing unchanged. See doc/troubleshooting/render_eta_probe_blowup.md. Commits cd9935d, 29daade.

Per-target deferred slot_tokens imports hoisted to once per sweep. Safety proof dev/scripts/verify_token_map_safety.py (write-set parity fresh/steady/undo, verdict SAFE_TO_IMPLEMENT); red-first regression guards in dev/tests/test_fast_sweep_tokens.py (steady sweep must do ZERO _slot_token_entry scans + stamp-parity tests). Measured: sweep p50 22.2 → 9.57 ms (−57%), UN/ASSIGN/SNAP identical (520 assigned). Suite 775 passed (the 19 test_preset_dirty fails are the known order-dependent set from 7cd68e6 — all 23 pass standalone). Roadmap notes: doc/roadmap/_dev/perf-hotpaths.md.   30. 🟢 The rest-drift indicator flags keyed-control drift, shows in Rest State Mode, and snaps keyed channels to their own key. get_rest_drifts (features/rest_state/logic.py) previously stood down wholesale in Rest State Mode (added 9917bc8) and only reported UNKEYED channels straying from the Rest Action — so with Value Lock pinning unkeyed values and rest mode suppressing the rest, the ghost drift indicator went dark in every mode the user works in. Rewrote it around one rule: a channel drifts when its live value matches NEITHER the Rest Action's frame-0 value NOR the assigned take's frame-0 key (pure decision _value_matches_any, 12 unit tests). Unkeyed channels — rest is the sole reference (unchanged, valid at any frame). Keyed channels — on-model at their own frame-0 key OR the rest pose; the keyed test runs in Rest State Mode (the flat rest pose holds at every frame) or at frame_current == 0 (off frame 0 a live take pose legitimately differs from frame 0, so keyed channels are skipped to avoid a playback false-positive flood). Because the mode assigns the Rest Action to every ad.action, the real take is recovered via resolve_cascade(CascadeProperty.ACTION, …, raw=True) and matched per target by slot name. Detection classifies drift via the raw take, but the revert DESTINATION follows the CURRENTLY-ASSIGNED action (ad.action — the Rest Action while the mode is on, the take otherwise) at frame 0, rest fallback: shared helper _revert_value(ad, …) feeds both snap_single_to_rest and the drift row's ref_val so the shown value equals the snap. In Rest State Mode a keyed transform (empties, lights) therefore reverts to REST, not the take — the raw resolver had wrongly sent it to the take's key (fixed 5841514); in a normal take a keyed channel still reverts to its own key, an unkeyed one to rest, and keyed-only channels the rest action doesn't cover can be snapped too. Classification helpers _build_raw_take / _take_action_slot / _take_keyed_value. Driver-driven channels are excluded from the scan (_driven_channels via animation_data.drivers, 2b7452e) — their live value is the driver expression, not the raw fcurve, which false-positived against rest (the Li_* light rigs); same exclusion the Value Lock engine makes, and it can't hide real drift. Remaining caveat: constraint / NLA-blend overrides are still unguarded (per-channel heuristics risk suppressing genuine drift; not observed). A per-scan force=True summary logs mode / frame / keyed+unkeyed / driven-excluded counts + example (topic UI·DRAW, gate-bypassing). Tests: test_rest_drift_keyed.py (19 — matches-any, take-resolution, driver-exclusion, revert-fallback) + test_rest_mode.py::test_rest_mode_rest_drift_producer_consults_mode rewritten from the old standdown assertion. Commits f2afddb, a053ebe, 2b7452e, 5841514. ✅ in-app verified.

  40. 🟢 Preloading no longer stutters the viewport between layer builds, and a build waits for about a second of complete stillness instead of firing the moment you pause. New process_monitor.signal_progress_redraw(include_viewport) tags only VIEW_3D UI (sidebar) regions per pass; the WINDOW region only when the overlay is on (overlay_position != 'OFF'), throttled to 2 Hz in vl_preload._signal_redraw. (2) One still probe (0.25 s) sufficed to build → the freeze landed the instant the user paused; now REQUIRED_STILL_CHECKS = 4 consecutive still probes (~1 s) via pure next_still_count() (any motion resets). The per-layer build freeze itself is Blender physics — a depsgraph build is atomic on the UI thread. Tests test_next_still_count_resets_on_change_and_grows_when_still, test_required_still_checks_is_about_a_second. Commit on fix/preload-idle-redraw.

(1) Nav-warning + inspector cache flushes became NAV_WARNINGS_INVALIDATE / INSPECTOR_CACHE_INVALIDATE emissions on core/event_bus.py; panels/navigation / panels/inspector self-subscribe invalidate_nav_warnings / mark_inspector_dirty at register time (12 upward core→panels imports removed, all fire-and-forget — commit d7bd6db). (3) The last ~23 core→feature return-value sites inverted onto the registry, each feature registering at enable time: rest_state (get_rest_action, rest_mode_action_name, is_rest_mode, get_rest_drifts, find_slot_collisions, the slot-snap trio, an arm_suppress mirror-suppress setter replacing external pokes at _suppress_until, invalidate_cascade_action_cache, rest-cache ensure/build/invalidate), render_presets (last_applied_preset, is_preset_dirty, find_missing_presets), still_mode (apply_still / apply_switch_snap / reconcile_sync bundle), navigation (get_slot_mismatches, formerly a private cross-package import — commit b5fcd8d). Hot paths (vl_switch_fast, the cascade-clear loop) hoist provider refs into locals once at setup — no query() in any inner loop; every site keeps its original try/except and a matching default so a torn-down / not-yet-loaded feature degrades exactly as the old ImportError skip did. Fast sweep UNREGRESSED: apply_vl_switch_unified clear-all warm p50 2.68 ms / cold p50 5.38 ms (main baseline 2.90 / 5.75; gate 8.98 ms).   54. 🟢 Internal: cache invalidation routed through a leaf registry — writers stop importing panel draw caches upward — no behavior change. Phase 2 of the decoupling plan. New utils/cache_registry.py (register_cache / unregister_cache / invalidate / invalidate_all / clear): each cache owner registers its invalidator under a topic at register time — navigation "nav_warnings" (invalidate_nav_warnings), inspector "inspector" (mark_inspector_dirty), context panel "vl_cascade" (invalidate_vl_cascade_cache), rest_state "rest_state" (cache.invalidate_all) — and 29 writer sites across 17 files (render_presets ×5 files, rest_state, bookmarks, variant_switch, operators/inspector ×8, operators/cascade ×3, operators/ui ×2, ops_slots_rename, ops_rule_logic, properties/_groups_shared, properties/tag_library, panels/context/properties, panels/inspector ×2) call invalidate("<topic>") on the leaf utils module instead of importing the owning panel upward. Registry semantics: idempotent registration, per-invalidator crash isolation (a broken cache never sinks the writer that announced a change), unknown topic = silent no-op (owner not yet registered → stale-cache degradation, matching the old except ImportError: pass behavior); clear() torn down in core/vl_handlers.py beside event_bus.clear() / providers.clear(). Division of labour vs the bus: event_bus = push notification with semantics, cache_registry = dumb pull-side invalidation fan-out — a writer uses one per concern. Tests: 9 pure registry tests + 4 bpy topic-wiring seam tests + a textual guard that the upward imports never regrow (dev/tests/test_cache_registry.py). Suite from main 1255 passed / 1 pre-existing skip. ARCH baseline re-frozen 193 → 185 keys, 0 new violations (auditor 9f113e2). Freeze gotcha for the record: --arch-freeze-baseline without AUDITOR_SCAN_ROOT scans zero files and writes an EMPTY baseline — always point it at the addon repo. Commit fe40bd4. ✅ in-app verified.

Commit 9503136.

  56. 🟢 Camera Visibility Automation caches the per-scene camera list instead of scanning scene.objects every switch. apply_camera_visibility (core/cascade.py) scanned all of scene.objects on every VL switch to find cameras, so switches slowed as a file accumulated cameras/objects — only with the automation on. New per-scene cache _camera_vis_cache = {scene_name: (object_count, (camera_name, …))}; the O(objects) scan reruns only when len(scene.objects) changed or a cached name no longer resolves to an in-scene camera (add / remove / rename / unlink — a same-count swap is still caught by the name check), otherwise the cached list is reused (O(cameras)). Invalidation policy is a pure, RED-first unit-tested core core/camera_vis_cache.py::camera_cache_valid (5 tests, dev/tests/test_camera_vis_cache.py). The cache lives behind the auto_camera_visibility early-return so the feature-off path stays zero-cost; names only, never object refs (stale-pointer ban). Also surfaced in the switch profiler (Added 26) so the fired/skip state + time are observable. Confirmed working in-app with the automation on. Troubleshooting doc/troubleshooting/camera_visibility_switch_slowdown.md. Commit 1047563.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
1. 🟢 Clearing all keyframes at the current frame now affects only the scene you're working in. PERF-03 review of the 12 bpy.data.objects for-loops surfaced one genuine over-reach: the keyframe-clear operator's use_all_targets branch (operators/inspector/ops_keyframes.py) built its global targets from context.scene (scene + scene.world + node trees) but then iterated bpy.data.objects for the per-object targets — so a "remove all keys at current frame" wiped keys on objects in every scene, inconsistent with its own scene-scoped Scene/World targets. Scoped the object walk to scene.objects. Strictly narrows the blast radius (can only clear ≤ what it did before); aligns the object scope with the already-scene-scoped global targets.

  2. 🟢 The View-Layer Preload panel no longer gets stuck open after a preload finishes. The nav-bar toggle renders only while _preload_show = is_preloading() or cold_count() > 0 (panels/navigation/panel.py), but the panel BODY drew on tks_show_preload_panel alone — so when a run completed (not preloading, 0 cold) the toggle vanished while the body kept drawing, stranding it open. Two-part fix: (a) the body draw is now gated on the same is_preloading() or cold_count() > 0 condition, so it auto-hides when there's nothing to show; (b) at natural queue-drain completion in core/vl_preload.py::_preload_tick, if cold_count() == 0, reset wm.tks_show_preload_panel = False so next time a layer goes cold the (red) badge re-appears closed rather than the panel silently re-opening. The toggle's update callback no-ops on a False write (and tks_show_preload_panel isn't self-mutually-exclusive on turn-off). See doc/troubleshooting/preload_panel_stuck_open.md.

  3. 🟢 A view layer visited by an interactive switch reverted to COLD in the preload panel. _preloaded_this_session had only two writers — reset_preload_state() and _preload_tick — and the tick excludes the active layer (pair == active_pair → continue). So nothing recorded a layer preloaded by switching, and the layer switched away from fell through ACTIVE→PRELOADED→PENDING to COLD despite its depsgraph still being cached. Fix: new vl_preload.mark_preloaded(scene, vl) (idempotent), called from core/vl_switch_fast.py::apply_vl_switch_unified — the single sweep every path funnels through (interactive / load-apply / batch / preloader), always for the layer being built. Deferred import (vl_preload imports vl_switch_fast → top-level would be circular). The load-apply records the initial active layer; each switch records the to-layer; the active-first check keeps the current layer showing ACTIVE. Tests: test_mark_preloaded_* in dev/tests/test_vl_preload.py. See doc/troubleshooting/preload_visited_layer_shows_cold.md.

  4. 🟢 The public roadmap is now a live board that keeps itself up to date, with a clearer layout. Source of truth stays in doc/roadmap/{ideas,active,done} (status = folder; _dev/ private). dev/scripts/build_wiki_roadmap.py rewritten: emits three Markdown tables (Feature · Meta · What) + per-feature pages; parse_meta now also reads the required **Size:** token; board_table collapses Theme/Area/Size into one Meta column of Material icons with { title= } tooltips, the feature title is the page link (no Link column), the size dot is :material-circle:{ .sz-* }; wrap_phases wraps ## Phases in a .phases div so CSS marks non-task <li> with an ⓘ glyph; board_blurb trims summaries; intro admonitions dropped; new --addon-root so the wiki CI runs it against the cloned .addon-src. .github/workflows/notify-wiki.yml now also triggers on doc/roadmap/** + the generator. doc/ROADMAP.md demoted to a pointer; every feature file gained a **Size:** token; doc/roadmap/README.md, the roadmap skill, and the global Auto-Push rule updated to the one-push flow (chose Option A — generate-in-CI — over moving the human source into the wiki).

  5. 🟢 VL-switch rest-snap silently skipped objects whose rest slots changed since the cache was built. apply_vl_switch_unified STEP 3 read get_cached_rest_values() behind only a lazy if _rest_cache is None guard; invalidate_rest_cache() is called only on rest-keyframe write/revert, so when the Rest Action's slot set changed any other way (e.g. snapshotting newly-selected objects) the cache stayed incomplete and those objects fell out of ref_by_slot → SNAP=0. A full Blender restart rebuilt the cache and masked it (diagnosis: pre-restart sweeps all SNAP=0; post-restart KEY_HITS=94, SNAP 2–28). Fix in features/rest_state/cache.py: new ensure_rest_cache_fresh() + pure _rest_cache_needs_rebuild(cache_present, live_names, cached_names) rebuild when the live Rest Action slot-name set diverges from _rest_slot_targets (add/remove/rename), not only when None; shared _slot_cache_name() helper (hoisted _ID_PREFIXES). core/vl_switch_fast.py calls it in place of the lazy build. See doc/troubleshooting/vl_switch_same_action_keyed_snap.md.

  6. 🟢 Same-action VL switch reset animated objects (notably Empties) to their Rest values. STEP 3's keyed-path exclusion matched the Rest action slot name against a keyed_paths set keyed by the cascade action slot name. Those names diverge for Empties — the Rest slot is {object} (ctrl_drawer_S_03) but STEP 2 renames the cascade slot to the template label {object}_EMPTY (ctrl_drawer_S_03_EMPTY) — so the keyed location path was not excluded and got snapped to rest; on a same-action switch the depsgraph never re-evaluates, so the bad snap stuck (laser trace: chosen_key='ctrl_drawer_S_03', name_in_ref=True label_in_ref=False, location[2] live=0.38379931 ref=0.37821015 willsnap=True, then stuck at the rest value). The manual path (get_rest_drifts/snap_single_to_rest) was always correct because it reads keyed paths from the target's actual assigned slot fcurves (identity). Fix in core/vl_switch_fast.py: build keyed_by_handle = {slot_handle: {(data_path, array_index)}} from the cascade action during the slot pre-scan; new pure helper keyed_paths_for_assigned_slot(keyed_by_handle, ad, target_action) matches by slot handle (rename/template-proof); STEP 3 skips those paths. Duplicate-slot theory ruled out (chosen_key and ref values were correct/stable). Tests: dev/tests/test_vl_switch_rest_snap.py (11 — handle exclusion incl. the Empty name-mismatch case + cache-freshness add/remove/rename); 152 adjacent rest/VL/slot tests still pass. See doc/troubleshooting/vl_switch_same_action_keyed_snap.md.

  7. 🟢 Naming-template rows in Preferences showed a doubled dropdown chevron. Each row's ▾ actions popover (draw_naming_template_row in preferences/draw_naming.py) is layout.popover(panel_id, text="", icon='DOWNARROW_HLT'). In the Preferences/Add-ons editor Blender draws a popover button's own native disclosure chevron whenever the button has spare width, so the explicit DOWNARROW_HLT icon (dim, left) and the native chevron (bright, right) both rendered — width-gated (it vanished as the window shrank and the button lost slack). The Properties editor (Smart Output popovers) doesn't add the extra chevron, so the byte-identical call is single there: an editor-surface behaviour, not the popover call/panel/layout. Ruled out (all confirmed not the cause): the compact scale_y on Slots-Object-Data, removing the icon (→ bare wide chevron), dropping align=True from the label/field split, changing the generated panels' bl_space_type/bl_region_type to 'PROPERTIES'/'HEADER' (user: "no change"), and restructuring to the Smart-Output flat-row layout (still doubled). Fix: pin the ▾ to a fixed icon-width cell so the editor can't give it spare width — pop = input_row.row(align=True); pop.ui_units_x = 0.8; pop.popover(...) — single clean ▾ at any window width. Diagnosed with a throwaway GUI-Blender screenshot probe (open real prefs → maximize → screenshot → zoom the button column), since the bug is invisible to headless tests. See doc/troubleshooting/naming_template_popover_double_chevron.md.

  8. 🟢 The File Name preview now resolves even when the Directory is invalid; a bad directory no longer collapses the Output panel. Root cause: the directory preview's else branch (taken only when the directory is valid) did a LOCAL from ..smart_syntax.engine import SmartSyntaxEngine, which makes the name local to the whole draw_output_panel_prepend function. When the directory was invalid that else was skipped, the local was never assigned, and the File Name preview's later use of SmartSyntaxEngine raised UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'SmartSyntaxEngine' → its try/except fell back to the raw, unresolved pattern ([view_layer]_####.[file_format]). Invisible headless (the bug is in panel-draw scope, not the resolver). Fix: drop the shadowing local imports (it's already imported at module scope). Found only via a diagnostic that surfaced the caught exception text in the preview. Several real but non-causal defensive fixes were made along the way (each made the failure degrade more gently, which is why the symptom kept moving): resolve_token/resolve_path now except Exception per-token (a cluster raising a non-(RuntimeError, AttributeError, KeyError) type no longer bubbles out and blanks the whole path — degrades just that token to [token]); the #### frame pass prefers the PASSED scene over bpy.context.scene and is guarded separately so a frame error can't discard token resolution; .alert moved from the directory preview box() to an inner row() (setting alert on a box() corrupted draw state and silently dropped the next box — the File Name preview); and the Output override wraps the prepend in try/except so a bad path can't drop the native Saving/image-settings section. Commits 07e66ea (the fix), ef32b48, dda7952, e99ddcf, 785bd5c. See doc/troubleshooting/smart_output_invalid_dir_filename_unbound.md. ✅ in-app verified.

  9. 🟢 Reset / Clear actions in the Build Syntax popover repaint the field, chips and preview immediately. Clicking Reset / Clear Last / Clear All changed wm.tks_token_builder but the keep-open popup only updated after a mouse move — Blender doesn't auto-redraw a floating keep-open popup when a child popover closes. The Clear and Reset operators now tag every editor for redraw so the popup repaints instantly (the final real Menu form also closes cleanly, repainting on close). Commit 8c43622. ✅ in-app verified.

  10. 🟢 Right-click → Online Manual reaches a feature page for ~100 more controls (INFO-04 wiki manual-map). Hand-wired the newer / namespaced operators and renamed redo-panel fields the AUTO-WIRED category pass in utils/manual_map.py misses, so the auditor's INFO-04 manual-map coverage dropped from 101 elements falling back to the wiki home to 4. New hand-maintained MANUAL_MAP entries (placed after the AUTO-WIRED block, before the safety-net catch-alls; lowercase, win-first) route: token builder + field_* → features/smart_output/; *output_rule* / rule-override → features/rules/; rq_* / render-queue / stat_info / detect_render_version / toggle_render_path_mode → features/batch_render/; pm_* / monitor-switch / profiler → features/process_monitor/; dm_* → preferences/debug/; tab/toggle_*_panel toggles + tree-design → interface/navigation_panel/; *tag_filter* → features/tags/; smart_undo/smart_redo → interface/hotkeys/; *action_name + toggle_autokey → interface/inspector_panel/; pies/keyframes → features/pie_menus/; toggle_version_warning → features/vl_versions/; *preload* / vl-settings copy-paste → interface/context_properties/; toggle_globals_mode → features/globals/; rebuild_cache → preferences/advanced/; plus per-class redo-field patterns for the renamed rest_state ops (snappropertytorest, unsetrestkeychannel, deleterestslot) the stale AUTO-WIRED *reference* entries no longer match. The four generic UI-list controls (tks.list_add/remove/move/clear) intentionally stay on the wiki home — reusable plumbing with no single page to document. Verified by replaying Blender's lowercase fnmatchcase matcher: 97 of 101 reroute, 0 uncovered, no test_manual_map.py contract violation.

  11. 🟢 The Take Name naming template kept reverting to an invalid value on fresh starts after a headless test-suite run (prefs autosave leak). Tests in dev/tests/test_token_builder.py pointed the Token Builder's WM target at the REAL naming_take_template pref; the live write-through (features/smart_syntax/properties.py::_builder_to_target) pushed fixtures (junk{scene} → [sep][hostname] → [scene][sep][take], last write wins) into AddonPreferences, and update=_autosave_preferences full-dumped the config over the developer's real user_preferences.json. Every GUI start then loaded the poisoned template (register-time load_saved_preferences() overrides both the property default and userpref.blend), the naming warning fired, and quit re-saved the value into userpref.blend — an in-app fix held only until the next test run. Fix: TKS_TEST_MODE env kill-switch in _autosave_preferences() (env var, not a flag — mid-suite addon_enable reloads prefs and would re-arm a Python flag), set by dev/tests/run_tests.py; snapshot/restore in the two prefs-targeting tests; syntax tests pin tks_token_target_kind='none'; new regression test dev/tests/test_prefs_autosave_guard.py asserts a pref write leaves the config byte-identical. Both stores repaired (JSON + headless save_userpref()). Commit 7cd68e6. See doc/troubleshooting/take_template_test_prefs_leak.md. ✅ in-app verified.

  12. 🟢 Calibrating render times no longer produces multi-minute estimates for takes that actually render in seconds. User-reported: 2 of 7 takes (~10s real render) showed ~22-minute estimates after Calibrate; the header ETA summed to ~24m. Three stacked root causes: (1) structural — the old probe extrapolated on pixels × nominal samples, which is ~100× wrong under Cycles adaptive sampling (see Changed 17 for the resolution-only probe redesign); (2) guard blindness — the probe sanity check read sibling reality from the session-only SKIP_SAVE tks_last_render_time, which is empty right after every restart, so stored garbage probes passed unchecked into the per-row labels AND the frozen batch snapshot (fixed by history-backed _vl_reality_seconds + the mid-batch reguard_estimate rescue, Changed 16); (3) verification blocked — a full day of in-app checks silently ran stale code because the Blender 5.1 extension junction pointed at the paused custom-tokens worktree (%APPDATA%…\extensions\user_default\takes_for_blender → .worktrees\Takes-for-Blender-custom-tokens); repointed to the main checkout — always verify the junction target before an in-app session. See doc/troubleshooting/render_eta_probe_blowup.md. Commits cd9935d, 29daade. ✅ in-app verified.

  13. 🟢 A background preload no longer overwrites camera, world, or action choices you made by hand. The restore leg re-applied the active layer's cascade resolution (_apply_preload_cascade(saved…)), not the actual prior state — manual deviations were stomped once per tick, and a layer resolving no camera CLEARED scene.camera (core/cascade.py None-branch, ~:548) / scene.world under auto-clear (~:635). core/vl_preload.py now captures a names-only raw snapshot per touched scene on first touch (_capture_scene_snapshot: camera/world/compositing_node_group/use_nodes + per-target (action.name, slot.identifier) keyed (obj.name, label) via iter_anim_targets) and the restore leg writes it back verbatim with guarded writes (pure restore_writes — equal values write nothing ⇒ nothing dirtied ⇒ restore-leg evaluated_depsgraph_get() dropped; target-leg eval kept — it IS the preload). Active snapshot recaptures when the user switches pairs mid-run (snapshot_needs_recapture). Names-only invariant enforced by non_name_values (no ID refs — PointerProperty-ban analog). start_preload also swapped the stale _rest_cache is None guard for ensure_rest_cache_fresh() (from 2ed1c31) so preload can't bake an incomplete rest cache. 7 TDD pure-helper tests in dev/tests/test_vl_preload.py. See doc/roadmap/_dev/preload-viewport-integrity.md. Commit e30b9d9.
  14. 🟢 Scenes shown in a second window are no longer switched around by a background preload. Only the user's scene+layer was restored; other scenes kept the last-preloaded VL's camera/world/compositor/actions. Every touched scene is now snapshot-captured on first touch and guarded-restored every tick (_restore_scene_snapshot(target_scene) first, then the active scene so shared objects end on the active layer's assignments); cancel / process-disable / batch-interrupt / addon-disable paths run _restore_all_snapshots(). Commit e30b9d9.
  15. 🟢 The VL Switch Profiler now repaints fully on an undo/redo re-fire (was single-writer + stale header + blank skipped groups). The profiler tree (wm.tks_profiler_items) had exactly one writer — the switch operator's deferred cascade in operators/context/ops_crud_switch.py — so the post-undo re-fire (core/vl_lifecycle.py::_maybe_refire_cascade_snap), which runs apply_vl_switch_unified directly and discarded its (_perf, _stat), left the panel showing the previous operator switch. Three stacked fixes: (1) extracted the ~90-line inline tree build into panels/navigation/profiler_feed.py::rebuild_profiler_tree(wm, perf, sweep_perf, sweep_stat, target_action_name, rest_action_name, ran) — ran (OPERATOR_PHASES vs SWEEP_ONLY_PHASES) marks phases the caller didn't execute as SKIPPED; the operator calls it with full groups (byte-parity), the re-fire with sweep-only (WM props aren't undo-tracked, so the deferred-timer write can't desync the undo stack — same pattern as the tks_cascade_step_* icons the sweep already sets there). (2) The header reads full_ms = tks_cascade_time_total + tks_cascade_time_eval, but tks_cascade_time_eval was written only by the operator, so a sweep-only rebuild left the prior switch's (dominant) eval time frozen; the helper now owns that prop (from perf['blender_eval'], 0.0 when absent) and the re-fire measures + times view_layer.update() when the profiler/cascade-steps panel is open (opt-in, safe in the deferred timer — the warm-up timer evals there too). Redraw switched to signal_redraw_deferred() because the profiler is a template_list and a synchronous tag_redraw from a timer skips the list's layout re-eval until a mouse move. (3) A SKIPPED group header rendered its timing cell blank (_draw_group only printed a time for exec_ms >= 0), reading as "not calculated"; extracted panels/navigation/monitors_profiler.py::group_timing_display(exec_ms, status) → dimmed "skipped" label + greyed header. Re-running overrides/finalize on undo is intentionally NOT done — apply_overrides() mutates materials/objects and fights Blender's native undo restore — so skipped is the correct state, now legible. Tests: dev/tests/test_profiler_feed.py (8), dev/tests/test_profiler_display.py (3), all red-first. Commits 821f266, 2b6569b, 0d92ea5. See doc/troubleshooting/profiler_stale_on_undo.md. ✅ in-app verified.
  16. 🟢 The public wiki deploys again on every push — two independent pipeline failures fixed. (1) dev/build/manifest_extractor.py classified classes by direct base name only (OPERATOR_BASES = {"Operator"}), so TKS_OT_BatchRenderBGFast(TKS_OT_BatchRenderBG) never reached ui_manifest.json; once the wiki documented tks.batch_render_bg_fast (June 29 bulk sprint), verify_wiki.py failed every deploy with "not in the addon manifest". Added an id-shape fallback (operator_shaped_idname: a literal dotted-lowercase bl_idname is unambiguously an operator) + same-file bl_label/bl_description inheritance mirroring Blender's runtime; pinned by dev/tests/test_manifest_extractor.py. (2) dev/scripts/build_wiki_roadmap.py mirrored card prose verbatim, so the preload-viewport-integrity card's ../done/vl-preload.md + ../_dev/preload-viewport-integrity.md links 404'd on the flat published board and failed mkdocs build --strict. New neutralize_relative_links() rewrites cross-card links to the published flat sibling when the slug is on the board and demotes the rest (typically _dev/ companions, excluded before the slug check to avoid self-links) to inline code — same guard the changelog mirror applies to .py links. Related: the auditor's scan_hub.py CONFIG_KEYS allowlist silently stripped every INFO-01 wiki config key (wiki_emit_audit_packets etc.), fixed in blender-addon-auditor 8d0117b. Deploy verified green (run 28900701075). See doc/troubleshooting/wiki_deploy_pipeline_failures.md. Commits 5451eeb, 186c416.
  17. 🟢 Same-action VL switch left a nudged keyed object at the nudge. Root cause (user-pinpointed via the Watchlist pipeline: "the snap is not firing between the same actions"): the fast sweep's STEP 2 writes ad.action = target_action only when the action CHANGES; that assignment write is what tags the target for animation re-evaluation, so a same-action switch performed no write, no re-eval happened, and a user-nudged keyed channel kept its nudge (different-action switches restore as a side effect of the write). STEP 3's rest snap can't cover it: it excludes every path the cascade action animates — and when the cascade action IS the rest action, that excludes everything. Fix: in the sweep's no-write branch, target.update_tag(refresh={'TIME'}) forces the same re-evaluation an action change causes; sweep summary log gains RETAG=N. Mechanism verified headless; confirmed in-app 2026-07-08. Works with the Value Lock off too. core/vl_switch_fast.py, commit ccac6d1. See doc/troubleshooting/vl_switch_same_action_no_reeval.md.
  18. 🟢 The headless test suite is hermetic from the developer's real prefs files — the 19 order-dependent test_preset_dirty/test_preset_compat failures (known since 7cd68e6) are gone. Root cause: two prefs-LOAD channels applied the real machine_state.json (presets_local_dir: "", MACHINE_SPECIFIC_KEYS #1) over the tmpdir the preset test modules seed at import — (1) test_output_popover_quickpick's autouse addon_enable re-running register() → load_saved_preferences() (the actual mid-suite trigger; test_output_* sorts before test_preset_*), and (2) the deferred load_post reload (_deferred_load_apply → load_project_preferences()) whenever timers pumped. TKS_TEST_MODE killed autoSAVES but not autoLOADS. Fix: symmetric kill-switches on both channels (preferences/persistence_io.py load_saved_preferences, core/vl_lifecycle.py deferred call site), mirroring the _autosave_preferences guard; direct monkeypatched load_project_preferences() calls in test_prefs_persistence.py stay live. Diagnosed with a temporary autouse conftest probe (before/after presets_local_dir per test) that pinpointed the flipping test in one run. Regression guard dev/tests/test_prefs_load_isolation.py (red-first: sentinel → ''). Suite 919 passed / 0 failed. See doc/troubleshooting/order_dependent_preset_test_failures.md. Commit 8332bff.
  19. 🟢 Deleting/unlinking the Rest State action while the mode is on now AUTO-EXITS cleanly (mode off, autokey + Value Lock restored, button grays); exiting the mode always restores both. Two-part follow-up to Added 21, with a UX revision superseding an interim red click-to-exit button (commit fd93fc3) via user-chosen auto-exit. (1) Auto-exit reconciler. No crash was ever possible — get_rest_action() is bpy.data.actions.get(name) (None on delete) and every rest read is None-guarded — but the mode went "zombie": flag on, autokey paused, resolver falling through to the real tree action. A deletion is first observed in draw / property-update contexts (mutation ban), so the fix is a one-shot timer: schedule_orphan_reconcile() (idempotent, gated on rest_mode_broken()) registers _reconcile_orphaned_rest_mode, which runs in a safe context and calls the shared deactivate_rest_mode(context, restore_lock=True) — set_rest_mode(False) + restore_autokey + _reapply_active_vl (reassigns the real tree actions) + Value-Lock restore. The reconciler defers (returns 0.15) while TKS_VL_STATE.addon_is_mutating/is_undoing/is_redoing, never throws into the timer loop, and is unregistered on addon unregister. Scheduled from two sites: the toggle's poll (per-redraw) on a mode-on orphan, and _on_rest_action_update (properties.py) for popover unlink/change. TKS_OT_RestModeToggle.poll now grays whenever not has_rest_action() (mode on OR off) — the prior "keep enabled to exit when broken" is obsolete since the reconciler auto-exits; the interim red alert= row and dynamic description() broken branch were removed. deactivate_rest_mode writes state DIRECTLY (not via the now-grayed toggle) so the auto-exit can't be blocked by its own poll. (2) Symmetric Value-Lock restore (unchanged from fd93fc3): entering sets Scene.tks_rest_mode_lock_was_on before releasing the lock; exit re-enables it after restore_autokey + reapply; recursion avoided via restore_lock: BoolProperty(default=True, SKIP_SAVE) (the Value-Lock reverse exclusion passes restore_lock=False). Ordering: restore_autokey (→ ON) then re-enable lock (→ re-pauses) reproduces the pre-mode state. Tests: test_rest_mode.py (pure — poll grays-always truth table, orphan-poll schedules reconcile, schedule idempotency + orphan-only, deactivate/schedule wiring, restore_lock+memory, reverse restore_lock=False) and test_rest_mode_sweep.py (registered end-to-end, @_needs_followup now also gates on deactivate_rest_mode: auto-exit-on-delete via the shared helper + lock round-trip). Junction serves MAIN → registered tests validate post-merge. Design record doc/roadmap/_dev/rest-state-mode.md. Worktree full suite 1027 passed / 0 failed; post-merge registered gate 3/3. Commits fd93fc3, f59a319.
  20. 🟢 A background preload no longer leaks another take's action onto your current take when you click an object afterwards. Log-diagnosed: the sweep writes iter_anim_targets(scene) + (scene.world) targets too (vl_switch_fast.py:581-582), but the raw snapshot only captured object walks — a world shader tree (SOLO_Nodes) stayed on the preloaded take's action (06-02_wheel), and the cascade handler's needs_cascade pass later wrote it onto the active layer on click. Fix e8fc971: shared _capture_targets/_restore_target_write (names-only, guarded), scene snapshot gains scene_targets, and NEW per-world _world_snapshots captured at the moment apply_overrides activates each layer's world (worlds are file-global; per-scene first-touch can't cover them), guarded-restored every tick + on cancel/disable. See doc/troubleshooting/preload_action_leak_nonobj_targets.md. Suite 1052/0.
  21. 🟢 While a preload run is live, working in the viewport no longer stutters between layer builds, and a stray action can no longer land on your current take mid-run. User pinpointed the timing (leak DURING the run, not on switches; transition lag at build handoff) — one mechanism: each build tick's depsgraph-update flood reaches handlers after is_applying releases; the inspector Pass-1 fresh-action scan (panels/inspector/sync_viewport.py) churned per-update cascade resolves every rest gap (lag) and its needs_cascade path (:245-267 → sync_actions.py:588 assign-to-VL) could fire off the flood (leak). Fix: _skip_fresh |= vl_preload.is_preloading() — mirrors the 0.5 s post-undo suppression below it; safe because the run holds Value Lock + autokey off, so no genuine fresh action can be authored mid-run; detection resumes at drain/cancel. NOTE also spotted for later: the sweep clears obj.tks_action when it equals the target action (vl_switch_fast.py:~553) — a preload can wipe a manual marker; not snapshot-covered yet. Commit on fix/preload-update-flood-gate. Suite 1053/0.
  22. 🟢 The "Pacing" preload option was invisible to the Preferences search. preload_pacing was never listed in prefs_search.SEARCH_INDEX (the Workflow>Automations "Actions" section carried preload_viewlayers_mode + preload_auto_open_panel only), so row_flags.get("preload_pacing", True) always defaulted to active — the row ignored the search filter (a §11.5 violation). Fixed by the new "Preload" SEARCH_INDEX section listing all three preload props. Commit 80375b6.
  23. 🟢 The viewport overlay mode-pill icons didn't dim while searching Preferences. The overlay_mode_* toggle row (draw_interface.py, Interface > Overlay > 3D Viewport) set no .active despite the props being indexed, so it stayed bright when the query matched nothing in it. Now pick.active = any(rf.get(p, True) for p in the five overlay_mode_* props). Commit 80375b6.
  24. 🟢 Config load could blank saved pie slots — dedup update-callbacks fired mid-load. _load_prefs_from_file applies saved slot values one setattr at a time and each fires the slot's update= dedup resolver (props_keyframe_pie._resolve_kf_pie_duplicates, props_f12_pie._resolve_f12_pie_duplicates, props_pie_menu._resolve_pie_duplicates); a saved value colliding with a not-yet-overwritten registration default (e.g. after 28b345b shipped East=NATIVE_INSERT) blanked the user's saved slot, and the next autosave persisted the loss — one mechanism behind "prefs keep changing after updates". All eight slot families (I/Alt, Still/Anim ×2 via _make_kf_updaters, F12, navigation) route through the three resolvers, which now early-return on is_prefs_loading() (the prefs_loading_guard the load already holds; same precedent as _on_prefs_filename_change). Interactive dedup unchanged. TDD: dev/tests/test_prefs_dedup_load_guard.py (12 param tests — RED reproduced the blanking in all families). Commit 628ffb1. See doc/troubleshooting/prefs_drift_settings_change_after_updates.md.
  25. 🟢 SHARED/LOCAL storage tiers with no folder silently fell back to the addon-default config file. _get_prefs_path() returns the default path when presets_shared_dir/presets_local_dir is empty — this machine ran prefs_save_mode=SHARED with an empty dir, so reads/writes went to the ADDON-tier file while the UI claimed Shared. New persistence_io.get_storage_fallback_warning() (re-exported via persistence) drawn as a wrapped_info_box(kind='status') in draw_data_storage.py beside the PROJECT missing-file warning; PROJECT/ADDON stay silent (own warning / is the fallback). Tests: dev/tests/test_prefs_storage_fallback_warning.py (3). Commit c54465c.

  26. 🟢 Dragging the timeline playhead while a Still take was active hard-crashed Blender (EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION @0x28).
